Primary GHS/UN hazard class of the chemical governed by this rule (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 5.1 Oxidizing Substances, Class 8 Corrosives, Class 6.1 Toxic Substances). description: Secondary hazard classification or division providing additional specificity within the primary hazard class (e.g., Division 1.1 for mass explosion risk, Packing Group I/II/III, sub-risk 6.1 on a Class 3 flammable). Captures multi-hazard chemical profiles per GHS and UN regulations.
reference: GHS Purple Book; UN Model Regulations; DOT 49 CFR 172.101

description: Primary hazardous consequence that would result from co-locating the two incompatible hazard classes defined in this rule. Used in risk assessment, PHA/HAZOP documentation, and emergency response planning. Informs the severity weighting in automated putaway violation alerts.

business_glossary_term: Incompatible Hazard Class — Side A (Segregation Matrix)
description: First hazard class in the incompatible pair defined by this segregation rule. Together with incompatible_hazard_class_b, forms the segregation matrix entry that prohibits co-location of chemicals from these two hazard classes in the same storage zone. Aligns with NFPA 400 Table 10.3.2 and UN segregation table.
reference: NFPA 400; UN Model Regulations Chapter 7.2; IMDG Code; DOT 49 CFR 177.848

business_glossary_term: Incompatible Hazard Class — Side B (Segregation Matrix)
description: Second hazard class in the incompatible pair defined by this segregation rule. Together with incompatible_hazard_class_a, forms the segregation matrix entry. The automated putaway validation engine in SAP EWM checks both directions of this pair to prevent co-location of incompatible chemicals.
reference: NFPA 400; UN Model Regulations Chapter 7.2; IMDG Code; DOT 49 CFR 177.848

description: Timestamp when this hazmat storage rule record was last updated. Supports change detection in downstream ETL pipelines and provides audit trail for rule modifications. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.

description: Date on which this storage rule was last reviewed for continued regulatory accuracy and operational relevance. ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 require periodic review of EHS controls. Triggers a review workflow in Intelex EHS Management when overdue.
reference: ISO 14001; ISO 45001; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; ACC Responsible Care

business_glossary_term: Maximum Storage Quantity Per Fire Zone (Kilograms)
description: Maximum allowable quantity in kilograms of chemicals governed by this rule that may be stored within a single fire zone or control area. Derived from NFPA 400 maximum allowable quantities (MAQ) tables and local fire code. Enforced by SAP EWM inventory capacity checks.
reference: NFPA 400 Table 4.3.1; IFC Chapter 50; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106

business_glossary_term: Maximum Storage Quantity Per Fire Zone (Liters)
description: Maximum allowable quantity in liters of liquid chemicals governed by this rule that may be stored within a single fire zone or control area. Complements the kilogram-based limit for liquid hazmat categories where volume is the regulatory basis (e.g., NFPA 30 flammable liquids).
reference: NFPA 30; NFPA 400; IFC Chapter 50; EPA 40 CFR Part 112

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for chemicals governed by this rule. Exceeding this threshold may cause decomposition, polymerization, or ignition. Monitored via Aveva PI System historian and triggers automated EHS alerts in Intelex.

description: Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for chemicals governed by this rule. Below this threshold, the chemical may solidify, become unstable, or lose efficacy. Monitored via Aveva PI System historian for real-time compliance alerting.
reference: GHS; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; ISO 9001; SDS Section 7

description: Date by which this storage rule must next be reviewed for regulatory currency and operational accuracy. Calculated based on review frequency policy (typically annual for high-hazard rules). Drives compliance calendar in Intelex EHS Management.

business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Fire Diamond Flammability Rating
description: NFPA 704 fire diamond flammability rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials that will rapidly vaporize at normal pressure and temperature. Used in fire zone maximum quantity calculations and emergency response planning.

business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Fire Diamond Health Rating
description: NFPA 704 fire diamond health hazard rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials that can cause death or major residual injury on short exposure. Used in PPE requirements and emergency response planning.

business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Fire Diamond Reactivity Rating
description: NFPA 704 fire diamond instability/reactivity rating (0–4) for chemicals governed by this rule. Rating 4 indicates materials capable of detonation or explosive decomposition at normal temperatures and pressures.

business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Fire Diamond Special Hazard Code
description: NFPA 704 special hazard symbol code for chemicals governed by this rule (e.g., W = water-reactive, OX = oxidizer, SA = simple asphyxiant, BIO = biological hazard, CRY = cryogenic). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1_explosive|class_2_gas|class_3_flammable_liquid|class_4_flammable_solid|class_5_oxidizer|class_6_toxic|class_7_radioactive|class_8_corrosive|class_9_misc — promote to reference product]
reference: DOT 49 CFR - Hazardous Materials Regulations; GHS - UN Recommendations on Transport of Dangerous Goods; NFPA 30; EPA RMP

description: Indicates whether the high-level alarm setpoint was active (True) or inactive (False) at the time of measurement. A high-level alarm signals that the tank level has reached or exceeded the high alarm threshold, requiring immediate operational attention to prevent overfill.
reference: IEC 61511 - Functional Safety; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; API 2350 - Overfill Protection

description: The P&ID instrument tag identifier for the level transmitter or gauge device that produced this measurement (e.g., LT-101, LIT-2204). Enables traceability to the physical instrument for calibration records, maintenance history, and instrument reliability analysis.
reference: ISA-5.1 - Instrumentation Symbols and Identification; P&ID Engineering Standards; SAP PM - Functional Location

description: The estimated financial value of the bulk chemical inventory in the tank at the time of measurement, expressed in US Dollars. Calculated from mass_kg and the current standard or moving average cost from SAP FI/CO. Used for inventory valuation reporting and financial close processes.
reference: SAP FI/CO - Inventory Valuation; FASB ASC 330 - Inventory; SOX Section 404

description: The measured liquid level height in the tank expressed in meters, as reported by the level transmitter instrument and captured in the Aveva PI System historian. This is the primary raw measurement value for continuous inventory tracking.
reference: Aveva PI System - Level Tag; ISA-75 Instrumentation Standards; API 650 Tank Standards

description: The current tank fill level expressed as a percentage of the tank's maximum working capacity (0-100%). Provides an intuitive operational view of tank utilization for dashboards, alerts, and capacity planning without requiring knowledge of tank dimensions.
reference: API 650; SAP EWM - Storage Bin Capacity; Aveva PI System

description: Indicates whether the low-level alarm setpoint was active (True) or inactive (False) at the time of measurement. A low-level alarm signals that the tank level has dropped to or below the low alarm threshold, indicating potential supply shortage or pump cavitation risk.
reference: IEC 61511 - Functional Safety; Honeywell Experion DCS - Alarm Management; ISA-18.2

description: The calculated mass of the liquid inventory in the tank expressed in kilograms, derived from volume and density. Used for material balance, production accounting, and regulatory emission calculations (e.g., EPA TRI, VOC reporting).
reference: EPA TRI Reporting; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; API MPMS Chapter 11

description: The data quality indicator for this measurement record as reported by the Aveva PI System historian (e.g., Good, Bad, Uncertain, Substituted). Reflects instrument health, communication status, or manual data substitution. Used to filter unreliable readings in analytics and reporting.
reference: Aveva PI System - Data Quality Codes; OPC UA Part 8 - Data Access Quality; ISA-88

description: Identifies the originating source system or method for this level measurement record. Distinguishes between automated PI historian readings, manual gauge readings entered by operators, DCS/SCADA system feeds, calculated values from strapping tables, or estimated values.
reference: Aveva PI System; Honeywell Experion DCS; Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7; ISA-88

description: The precise date and time at which the tank level measurement was recorded by the process historian or instrumentation system. This is the principal real-world event time for this observation record.
reference: Aveva PI System - Tag Timestamp; ISA-88 Batch Control Standard

description: The Aveva PI System historian tag identifier (e.g., SITE01.TK-101.LEVEL) that is the source of this measurement record. Enables traceability back to the originating instrument data stream in the process historian.
reference: Aveva PI System - Tag Configuration; ISA-88

description: The date and time when this tank farm level record was first created or ingested into the Silver Layer data lakehouse. Distinct from measurement_timestamp (the real-world event time). Used for data pipeline auditing, latency monitoring, and record lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 9001 - Record Management; Databricks Lakehouse - Silver Layer Audit

description: The date and time when this tank farm level record was last modified in the Silver Layer, for example due to a data quality correction, manual override, or reprocessing event. Supports change tracking and data lineage auditing.
reference: ISO 9001 - Record Management; Databricks Lakehouse - Silver Layer Audit

description: The inventory mass threshold in kilograms at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this tank. When mass_kg falls at or below this value, procurement or supply chain teams are alerted to initiate a purchase order or transfer. Supports continuous inventory management for bulk chemicals.
reference: SAP MM - MRP Reorder Point Planning; SAP EWM - Replenishment Management

description: The maximum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in this tank, expressed in degrees Celsius, as specified in the material SDS (Safety Data Sheet). Exceeding this threshold may indicate a safety hazard, product degradation risk, or regulatory non-compliance.
reference: GHS - Safety Data Sheet Section 7; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM; NFPA 30 - Flammable Liquids Code

description: The minimum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in this tank, expressed in degrees Celsius, as specified in the material SDS (Safety Data Sheet). Used to trigger temperature deviation alerts and ensure regulatory compliance with safe storage requirements.
reference: GHS - Safety Data Sheet Section 7; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM; NFPA 30 - Flammable Liquids Code

description: The nominal or maximum working capacity of the tank in cubic meters as defined in the tank design specification and strapping table. Stored with the measurement record to enable percentage calculations and capacity utilization reporting without requiring a join to the tank master.
reference: API 650 - Welded Tanks for Oil Storage; ISO 7507 - Tank Calibration; SAP PM - Equipment Master

description: The name or designation of the tank farm area or storage cluster where the tank is physically located (e.g., North Tank Farm, Solvent Storage Area). Supports area-level inventory aggregation and reporting.
reference: SAP EWM - Warehouse Structure; EPA RMP - Facility Siting

description: The human-readable functional identifier of the storage tank as designated in the plant P&ID (Piping and Instrumentation Diagram) and SAP PM equipment master (e.g., TK-101, T-2204). Used for operational reference and reporting.
reference: SAP PM - Equipment Master; P&ID Engineering Standards; ISA-5.1

description: The temperature of the liquid in the tank at the time of measurement, expressed in degrees Celsius. Temperature is required for density correction, volume-to-mass conversion, and monitoring of temperature-sensitive chemicals to ensure safe storage conditions.
reference: Aveva PI System - Temperature Tag; API MPMS Chapter 11.1 - Temperature Correction; OSHA PSM

description: Indicates whether the measured temperature at the time of this record was outside the defined safe storage temperature range (True = deviation detected, False = within range). Supports EHS compliance monitoring and automated alerting for temperature-sensitive chemical storage.
reference: OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; GHS SDS Section 7; Intelex EHS Management

description: The available headspace (ullage) in the tank expressed in meters, calculated as the difference between the maximum allowable working level and the current measured level. Critical for safe filling operations, overfill prevention, and scheduling inbound deliveries.
reference: API 650 - Welded Tanks for Oil Storage; OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119; EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68

description: The available headspace volume in the tank expressed in cubic meters, representing the remaining capacity available for additional material. Used for logistics planning, delivery scheduling, and overfill risk management.
reference: API 650; EPA RMP 40 CFR Part 68; SAP EWM - Capacity Management

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inventory value is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR). Supports multi-currency reporting for global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes; FASB ASC 830 - Foreign Currency; SAP FI/CO

description: The calculated volume of liquid in the tank expressed in liters, derived from the level measurement and tank strapping table. Used for inventory quantity reporting and material balance calculations.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 2 - Tank Calibration; SAP MM - Inventory Management

description: The calculated volume of liquid in the tank expressed in cubic meters (m³). Provides SI unit representation for regulatory reporting, engineering calculations, and international trade documentation.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 2; ISO 7507 - Calibration of Vertical Cylindrical Tanks; EPA Tier II Reporting

description: Primary key for count_session
Identifier of the employee responsible for conducting the count.
Self-referencing FK on count_session (recount_count_session_id)
Work shift during which the count session was performed.
Identifier of the warehouse or tank farm where the count took place.
Free‑form notes entered by the counter about observations or issues.
Regulatory compliance outcome of the count session.
Classification of the count session (e.g., full inventory, cycle count, spot check).
Timestamp when the count session record was first created in the system.
Identifier of the handheld scanner or device used for the count.
Timestamp when the count session was completed or stopped.
Earliest expiration date among items counted, used for shelf‑life monitoring.
Recorded humidity level in the counting area, relevant for hygroscopic chemicals.
Specific area, aisle, or zone within the warehouse being counted.
Indicates whether the counted inventory was placed under quarantine.
Range of lot numbers included in this count session, if applicable.
Technique used to capture inventory quantities.
True if a regulatory hold was placed on the inventory after counting.
External reference number assigned to the count session for tracking.
Timestamp when the count session began.
Current lifecycle state of the count session.
Recorded ambient temperature in the counting area, relevant for temperature‑sensitive materials.
Number of distinct inventory items counted during the session.
Number of distinct inventory items expected to be present before counting.
Total quantity measured during the count session.
Total expected quantity (e.g., kilograms, liters) of inventory before the count.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the count session record.
Percentage variance between expected and counted quantities.
Difference between expected and counted quantities.
Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory adjustment record in the Chemical Mfg lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Adjustments affect financial valuation per product; linking to chemical_product enables audit trails, cost allocation, and regulatory impact analysis.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links adjustments to cost centers for variance analysis and internal cost control reporting.
Foreign key linking to maintenance.equipment. Business justification: Adjustments caused by equipment failures or repairs; needed for audit trail linking inventory adjustments to affected equipment.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Ensures each inventory adjustment posts to the appropriate GL account for financial statements and audit trails.
Reference to the specific production or procurement lot (batch) whose stock is being adjusted. Critical for FIFO/FEFO lot rotation compliance and traceability in chemical manufacturing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Batch Management; GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice)

description: Reference to the material (raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material) whose stock quantity is being adjusted. Corresponds to the SAP MM material master record.

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original inventory adjustment record that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag is True. Enables full reversal chain traceability for audit and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); SAP S/4HANA MM – Reversal

description: Reference to the employee (warehouse operator, inventory controller, or production planner) who initiated and submitted the inventory adjustment request. Required for SOX dual-authorization controls and audit trail.
Reference to the warehouse storage location, tank farm, or bin where the adjusted stock resides. Integrates with SAP EWM for warehouse operations and hazardous material storage compliance.
reference: SAP EWM (Extended Warehouse Management); SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Current lifecycle state of the inventory adjustment record, tracking progression from initial draft through dual-authorization approval (required for SOX-controlled threshold adjustments) to final posting or reversal in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Internal Controls

description: The principal real-world business event timestamp recording when the inventory adjustment was physically executed or the stock correction was applied. Distinct from the record creation audit timestamp. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Posting Date; ISO 9001

description: Classification of the inventory adjustment indicating the business reason category: write-off (stock reduction for loss/destruction), write-up (stock increase for found goods), count_variance (physical inventory count discrepancy posting), damage (goods damaged in storage or handling), obsolescence (expired or out-of-spec material), or revaluation (value correction). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: write_off|write_up|count_variance|damage|obsolescence|revaluation|transfer_correction|system_correction — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Movement Types; ISO 9001 Inventory Control

description: The calendar date on which the inventory adjustment received its final required authorization. Used for SOX compliance reporting and aging analysis of pending adjustments. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

business_glossary_term: CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) Reference Number
description: Reference number linking this inventory adjustment to an associated CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) record when the adjustment triggers or is part of a quality or process improvement action. Supports ISO 9001 quality management traceability.
reference: ISO 9001 – CAPA; LabWare LIMS; Intelex EHS Management

description: Audit timestamp recording when this inventory adjustment record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, SOX audit trails, and lakehouse ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the financial impact value is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency financial reporting and consolidation.

Externally-known business identifier for the inventory adjustment document, typically matching the SAP MM material document number generated at the time of posting. Used for cross-referencing with ERP and audit trails.

business_glossary_term: EHS (Environment Health and Safety) Incident Reference Number
description: Reference number linking this inventory adjustment to an associated EHS incident record in Intelex EHS Management (e.g., a spill, fire, or contamination event that caused material loss). Null when the adjustment is not related to an EHS incident.
reference: Intelex EHS Management; OSHA; EPA; ISO 45001

description: The monetary value impact of the inventory adjustment on the balance sheet, calculated as adjustment quantity multiplied by the standard or moving average price of the material. Positive for write-ups, negative for write-offs. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) reporting and SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO – Material Valuation; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FASB ASC 330 Inventory

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the material being adjusted is classified as hazardous under GHS, OSHA, or DOT regulations. When True, additional EHS (Environment Health and Safety) reporting and disposal compliance requirements apply to the adjustment.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System); OSHA; DOT (Department of Transportation); TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act)

description: Human-readable description of the material being adjusted, as maintained in the SAP MM material master. Supports reporting and analytics without requiring a join to the material master table.

SAP MM material number (alphanumeric code) identifying the specific chemical, raw material, intermediate, or finished good subject to the inventory adjustment. Enables direct cross-reference to SAP S/4HANA without relying solely on the surrogate key.

SAP MM movement type code that classifies the goods movement transaction (e.g., 551 for scrapping, 701 for physical inventory difference posting). Determines the accounting entries and stock update logic in SAP.

Free-text field for additional operational notes, instructions, or context provided by the requestor or approver regarding the inventory adjustment. Supports operational communication and audit documentation.

SAP MM physical inventory document number associated with this adjustment when the adjustment originates from a physical inventory count (cycle count or annual count). Null for non-count-related adjustments such as damage write-offs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Physical Inventory; ISO 9001

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing site or distribution center where the inventory adjustment is being made. Supports multi-site inventory management and plant-level financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Plant; ISO 14001 Site Management

description: The accounting posting date on which the inventory adjustment is recorded in the general ledger and stock accounts in SAP FI/CO. May differ from the adjustment timestamp if adjustments are posted retroactively within an open period. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO – Posting Date; FASB ASC 330

description: The quantity of material being adjusted, expressed as a positive value for stock increases (write-ups, found goods) or negative value for stock reductions (write-offs, damage, count shortages). Stored in the base unit of measure of the material.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Goods Movement; ISO 9001 Inventory Control

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the material lot being adjusted was in quarantine status (OOS – Out of Specification or OOT – Out of Trend hold) at the time of the adjustment. Supports QM (Quality Management) traceability and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM – Quality Management; GMP; ISO 9001

description: Standardized reason code classifying the root cause or business justification for the inventory adjustment (e.g., PHYS_INV_DIFF for physical inventory difference, DAMAGE_STORAGE for storage damage, EXPIRY for expired material). Drives financial posting rules and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Reason Codes; ISO 9001 CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action)

description: Free-text narrative description providing additional context for the inventory adjustment beyond the standardized reason code. Captures specific circumstances such as spill details, damage cause, or discrepancy explanation for audit and CAPA purposes.

Boolean flag indicating whether this inventory adjustment record is a reversal of a previously posted adjustment. True = this record reverses a prior adjustment; False = original adjustment. Supports audit trail integrity and double-entry accounting reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Reversal; SOX; FASB

description: The SAP MM material document number generated when the inventory adjustment is posted in SAP S/4HANA. Serves as the primary cross-reference between the lakehouse silver layer record and the ERP system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.

The fiscal year associated with the SAP MM material document number. Required because SAP material document numbers are unique only within a fiscal year; combined with sap_material_document_number provides a globally unique ERP reference.

business_glossary_term: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Dual-Authorization Threshold Exceeded Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether the financial impact value of this adjustment exceeds the SOX dual-authorization threshold, triggering the requirement for a secondary approver. True = dual authorization required and applied; False = single authorization sufficient.
The monetary threshold value above which a second approver is required per SOX internal control policy. Stored on the record for audit traceability as thresholds may be updated over time.

SAP storage location code (e.g., warehouse zone, tank farm area, quarantine area) within the plant where the adjusted material is stored. Supports hazardous material segregation compliance and EWM warehouse operations.
reference: SAP EWM; SAP S/4HANA MM; OSHA PSM (Process Safety Management)

description: The unit of measure in which the adjustment quantity is expressed (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for liters, MT for metric tons, DRUM for drum containers). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure for the material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|EA|BAG|DRUM|IBC|TON — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Unit of Measure; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Audit timestamp recording when this inventory adjustment record was last modified, supporting change tracking and SOX dual-authorization audit requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The unit price (standard cost or moving average price) used to calculate the financial impact value of the adjustment at the time of posting. Captured for audit traceability as prices may change over time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO – Material Valuation; FASB ASC 330

description: Primary key for the LotAllocation association
Foreign key linking to the invoice line
Foreign key linking to the lot
Manufacturing batch or lot identifier associated with the product. [Moved from invoice_line: Current FK storing lot identifier belongs in the association as the link to lot]
Amount of material from the lot allocated to the invoice line
Unit of measure for the allocated quantity
Unique surrogate identifier for each physical inventory count record in the lakehouse silver layer. Maps to SAP MM physical inventory document item.
Employee identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved the variance write-off. Required for SOX segregation of duties — the approver must be different from the counter.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA MM Approval Workflow

description: Identifier grouping multiple count document items that were counted together as part of the same physical inventory session or cycle count run. Enables session-level reporting and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; SAP EWM Cycle Counting

description: Employee identifier of the person who physically performed the count. Required for accountability, SOX segregation of duties, and audit trail. References the workforce/HR system.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Physical inventory count should reference the stock position record that aggregates quantity and location; removes duplicated attributes.
Free-text notes entered by the approver explaining the rationale for approving the variance write-off. Supports audit documentation and Root Cause Investigation (RCI) for recurring discrepancies.
Date and time when the variance write-off was approved by the authorized approver. Part of the SOX-compliant approval chain audit trail for inventory reconciliation.

System-recorded (book) quantity of the material at the storage location at the time the count document was created, as per SAP MM inventory records. Represents the expected quantity before physical verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Inventory Management; FASB ASC 330

description: Calendar date on which the physical count was performed. Used for inventory valuation cutoff, regulatory reporting, and SOX period-end reconciliation.

business_glossary_term: Physical Inventory Count Document Item Number
description: Line item number within the SAP physical inventory document, distinguishing individual material-storage location combinations within the same count document.
SAP MM physical inventory document number (MI01/MI04) that uniquely identifies the count event in the source system. Used for reconciliation and audit trail back to SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory (Transaction MI01)

description: Indicates whether the count was performed blind (counter not shown book quantity, reducing bias) or informed (counter shown book quantity). Blind counting is preferred for SOX-compliant inventory reconciliation.

Current lifecycle status of the count document: open (count not yet performed), counted (count entered, pending posting), recounted (recount completed), posted (variance posted to inventory), cancelled (count voided).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory Workflow

description: Precise date and time when the physical count was recorded, including timezone offset. Used for intraday reconciliation and audit trail in continuous process manufacturing environments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; SOX Audit Trail

description: Classification of the count event: cycle_count (periodic scheduled count of a subset of materials), annual_wall_to_wall (full facility count), triggered_count (initiated due to discrepancy or event), or recount (second count to resolve variance).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; SOX Inventory Controls

description: Actual physical quantity of the material counted and recorded by the counter. This is the primary measurement outcome of the count event.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory (Transaction MI04); ISO 9001

description: Full name of the employee who performed the physical count. Stored for audit readability and SOX compliance reporting without requiring a join to HR systems.
Timestamp when the physical inventory count record was first created in the source system (SAP MM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX record retention compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the inventory valuation amount (e.g., USD, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global chemical manufacturing sites.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; FASB ASC 830 Foreign Currency

description: Expiration or shelf-life expiry date of the lot/batch being counted. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation compliance and identification of expired stock during physical counts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management; GMP Shelf Life Management; FEFO

description: Accounting period (month) within the fiscal year during which the count was performed (e.g., 001 through 012). Used for period-end inventory cutoff and financial close processes.
reference: FASB ASC 330; SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period

description: Fiscal year in which the physical inventory count was performed. Used for period-end financial reporting, SOX compliance, and year-over-year inventory accuracy trend analysis.
reference: FASB ASC 330; SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year

description: Indicates whether the material being counted is classified as hazardous under GHS, OSHA, or DOT regulations. Drives additional safety protocols during physical counting and storage compliance checks.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; DOT 49 CFR; TSCA

description: Monetary value of the inventory variance calculated as variance_quantity multiplied by the standard or moving average price of the material. Used for financial impact assessment and COGS adjustment. Expressed in local currency.
reference: FASB ASC 330; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Inventory Valuation; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the physical inventory count record. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and change data capture tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Short description of the material being counted as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable context for reporting without requiring a join to the material master.

SAP material document number generated when the inventory variance is posted (Transaction MI07). Links this count record to the resulting inventory_adjustment material document for financial reconciliation.
Accounting date on which the inventory variance was posted to the general ledger in SAP FI/CO. Critical for period-end cutoff, COGS calculation, and SOX financial reporting compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; FASB ASC 330; SOX Section 404

description: Status of the variance posting to the SAP general ledger: not_posted (pending), posted (successfully posted), posting_failed (error during posting), reversed (posting reversed for correction).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI Integration; SOX Section 404

description: Stock status of the material at time of count: unrestricted (available for use), quarantine (held pending investigation), blocked (restricted from use), quality_inspection (held for QC testing). Aligns with SAP MM stock categories.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Types; GMP Quality Hold Procedures; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether a recount has been triggered for this count item, typically due to variance exceeding the tolerance threshold. True = recount required or performed; False = no recount needed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; SOX Inventory Controls

description: Sequential number indicating which recount iteration this record represents (1 = first recount, 2 = second recount, etc.). Zero or null indicates the original count. Supports multi-round recount audit trails.

Indicates the ownership or allocation type of the stock being counted: own (company-owned), consignment (customer-owned at our facility), project (allocated to a project), sales_order (reserved for a sales order), vendor_consignment (vendor-owned at our facility).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Special Stock Management

description: Unit of measure in which the book, counted, and variance quantities are expressed (e.g., KG, L, MT, EA, DR for drums). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure from the material master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISO 80000 Units

description: Variance quantity expressed as a percentage of book quantity. Used to determine whether variance exceeds tolerance thresholds requiring management approval or recount. Key KPI for inventory accuracy reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; SOX Inventory Controls

description: Difference between counted quantity and book quantity (counted_quantity minus book_quantity). Positive value indicates surplus; negative value indicates shortage. Drives inventory adjustment postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; FASB ASC 330; SOX Section 404

description: Allowable variance percentage threshold configured for this material or material group. Variances exceeding this threshold require management approval for write-off. Supports SOX-compliant variance authorization controls.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory Tolerance Groups

description: SAP EWM warehouse number identifying the logical warehouse complex. Used when the count is managed at the warehouse management level rather than simple storage location level.

Indicates whether the variance percentage is within the configured tolerance threshold. True = variance is acceptable and can be auto-posted; False = variance exceeds tolerance and requires manual approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; SOX Section 404

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory movement event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the stock_movement data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Document

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for posting inventory movements to the correct cost center in the Cost Center Allocation report.

The SAP user ID (USNAM) of the person who posted the inventory movement in SAP S/4HANA MM. Critical for audit trail, SOX compliance, and accountability tracking. Represents the system user, which may be a human operator or an automated interface (e.g., MES integration, SCADA feed).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - USNAM; SOX; ISO 9001; GMP 21 CFR Part 11

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Stock movement must be linked to the lot it moves; replace string lot_number with FK lot_id for traceability.

Foreign key linking to order.outbound_delivery. Business justification: Required for the Delivery Execution report to trace which stock movements belong to each outbound delivery for compliance and audit.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports profit center profitability analysis of inventory movements for internal management reporting.
Foreign key linking to inventory.transfer_order. Business justification: Link stock movement to its originating transfer order for end‑to‑end tracking; replace free‑text field with FK.
Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Stock movement must reference the source warehouse location entity for accurate location tracking and audit reporting.

The exact date and time when the stock movement record was first captured and persisted in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and FIFO/FEFO sequencing validation. Corresponds to SAP CPUDT + CPUTM fields combined.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - CPUDT/CPUTM; SOX; ISO 9001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the movement_amount valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Corresponds to SAP WAERS field. Required for multi-currency chemical manufacturing operations with global procurement and sales.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - WAERS; ISO 4217; FASB; IFRS IAS 21

description: The SAP storage location code (Lagerort) to which the material is being moved or received. Represents the target warehouse area, tank farm zone, production staging area, or quarantine location. Null for pure outbound goods issues with no destination location within the plant.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - LGORT (destination); SAP EWM; OSHA PSM

description: The date on the physical or external source document (e.g., vendor delivery note, production order confirmation) that triggered the inventory movement. May differ from posting_date when documents are processed with a lag. Corresponds to SAP BLDAT field.

The expiration or shelf-life end date of the lot/batch being moved. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation enforcement, quarantine triggering for expired materials, and regulatory compliance. Corresponds to SAP VFDAT (shelf life expiration date) on the batch master. Critical for chemical raw materials, intermediates, and finished goods with defined shelf lives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - VFDAT; GMP; FDA 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001; REACH

description: The result of the FIFO (First In First Out) or FEFO (First Expired First Out) lot rotation validation check performed at the time of goods issue. Passed = correct lot selected per rotation policy; Failed = incorrect lot selected, violating rotation rules; Not Applicable = movement type does not require lot rotation validation (e.g., goods receipt); Overridden = FIFO/FEFO rule was manually overridden with documented justification. Critical for chemical product quality, shelf-life management, and GMP compliance.
reference: SAP EWM; SAP S/4HANA MM; GMP; FDA 21 CFR Part 211; ISO 9001

description: Externally-known reference number of the consignment agreement governing this vendor-owned stock arrangement. Links to the contractual terms under which the vendor retains ownership until consumption trigger is met. Sourced from SAP MM purchasing info record or outline agreement.

description: Reference number of the vendor-provided Certificate of Analysis (COA) accompanying the consignment lot. Confirms the material meets agreed quality specifications. Linked to LabWare LIMS for incoming quality verification.

description: Current quantity of vendor-owned consignment stock on hand at the manufacturer's premises, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents the unreceived (not yet financially owned) stock balance.

description: Current lifecycle status of the consignment stock record. 'active' = vendor-owned stock available for consumption; 'consumed' = fully consumed and liability transferred; 'partially_consumed' = partially drawn down; 'returned' = returned to vendor; 'expired' = past expiration date; 'quarantine' = held pending quality disposition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Special Stock (MSKU); ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Total financial value of the current consignment stock on hand, calculated as consignment_quantity multiplied by valuation_price. Used for balance sheet disclosure of vendor-owned stock, financial reporting, and off-balance-sheet liability tracking under FASB ASC 330 and SOX.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Inventory Valuation; FASB ASC 330; SOX

description: Cumulative quantity of consignment stock that has been consumed (goods-issued to production or other cost centers) and for which financial liability has been transferred to the manufacturer. Used for settlement reconciliation and VMI performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Consignment Goods Issue (MIGO 201K); MRKO Settlement

description: The agreed business event that triggers financial liability transfer from vendor to manufacturer for consignment stock. Defines when ownership and payable obligation are created. Aligned with SAP MM consignment settlement process.

description: Timestamp when the consignment stock record was first created in the system, typically upon goods receipt posting in SAP MM. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting compliance.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the valuation price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange revaluation under FASB and SOX.

description: Date after which the consignment material is no longer suitable for use per quality specifications or regulatory requirements. Drives FEFO lot rotation, quarantine triggers, and vendor return decisions. Critical for GMP and chemical stability compliance.

description: SAP material document number generated when the consignment stock was physically received at the plant (MIGO goods receipt against consignment PO). Used for traceability and audit trail of stock movements.

description: Indicates whether the consignment stock material is classified as hazardous under GHS, DOT, or OSHA regulations. Drives storage compliance requirements, SDS documentation obligations, and EHS reporting under TSCA and REACH.

description: Date of the most recent goods issue (consumption) from this consignment stock lot. Used for slow-moving stock analysis, VMI replenishment planning, and consignment agreement compliance monitoring.

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the consignment stock record (e.g., quantity adjustment, status change, quarantine flag update). Supports data lineage, change audit, and Silver layer delta processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.

description: Date on which financial ownership and payable liability for the consignment stock transferred from the vendor to the manufacturer, triggered by the agreed consumption event. Used for accounts payable accrual and financial period-end reporting under SOX and FASB ASC 330.

description: Vendor-assigned or internally assigned batch/lot number for the consignment stock. Critical for FIFO/FEFO lot rotation, traceability, Certificate of Analysis (COA) linkage, and regulatory compliance under GMP and REACH.

description: Maximum number of days the consignment stock may be held at the manufacturer's premises per the consignment agreement before automatic return or forced consumption. Breach of this threshold may trigger penalty clauses or mandatory settlement.

description: SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing site or facility where the consignment stock is physically located. Used for plant-level inventory reporting and regulatory site compliance.

description: Specific warehouse bin, tank number, or silo identifier within the storage location where the consignment material is physically stored. Supports hazardous material segregation and OSHA PSM compliance.

description: Maximum permitted storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the consignment material as specified in the SDS or vendor COA. Critical for flammable, reactive, or thermally unstable chemical feedstocks.

description: Minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the consignment material as specified in the SDS or vendor COA. Ensures compliant storage conditions for temperature-sensitive chemicals and prevents degradation or hazardous reactions.

description: Base unit of measure for the consignment quantity (e.g., KG for kilograms, L for litres, MT for metric tonnes, DRUM for drum containers). Actual date on which the goods issue posting was made in SAP MM, confirming physical departure of material from the sending plant. Used for transit time analysis and on-time shipment KPI reporting.

Actual date on which the goods receipt posting was made in SAP MM at the receiving plant, confirming physical arrival and acceptance of the transferred material. Used for transit time calculation and inventory reconciliation.

Carrier-assigned reference number (e.g., bill of lading number, waybill number, or pro number) for the shipment. Used to cross-reference with Oracle Transportation Management and carrier tracking systems.
SAP user ID of the person who created the stock transfer order in SAP S/4HANA. Required for audit trail, authorization control, and SOX compliance documentation.
Timestamp when the stock transfer order was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Represents the business event of order initiation, not the ETL load time.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transfer value (e.g., USD, EUR). Required for multi-currency inter-company transfer valuation and financial reporting under FASB and SOX.
SAP outbound delivery document number created in SAP EWM/SD for the physical shipment execution of this stock transfer order. Links the STO to the warehouse execution and shipping documents.

Shelf-life expiration date of the transferred lot. Governs FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation logic and ensures expired or near-expiry materials are flagged before transfer. Mandatory for regulated chemicals and pharmaceutical-grade materials.

description: Flag indicating whether the transferred material is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, labeling, and transport documentation under DOT, GHS, and IMDG regulations. Triggers mandatory SDS/MSDS documentation and emergency response procedures.

description: Quantity of material currently in transit between the sending and receiving plants — goods issued but not yet goods-receipted. Represents the stock-in-transit position used for real-time inventory visibility and financial valuation of in-transit inventory.

Actual quantity of material physically issued (goods issued) from the sending plant's inventory at the time of transfer. May differ from transfer_quantity due to partial shipments or measurement variances.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-Goods Issue; SAP EWM

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the stock transfer order record in the source system. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing.
Short descriptive name of the material being transferred as maintained in the SAP material master. Provides human-readable identification for operational and compliance reporting.

SAP material master number uniquely identifying the chemical, raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging material being transferred. Serves as the primary material reference across SAP MM, PP, and QM modules.
Planned date on which goods are scheduled to be issued from the sending plant's inventory. Derived from the requested delivery date and transit lead time in SAP MM planning.

Business priority level assigned to the stock transfer order, used to sequence fulfillment and logistics scheduling. Urgent transfers may be triggered by production line stoppages or critical inventory shortfalls.

SAP purchase order number associated with this stock transfer order for cross-company or third-party warehouse transfers. Links the STO to the procurement document for financial settlement and accounts payable processing.

Quality management status of the transferred material lot indicating whether it is available for unrestricted use or held under quarantine/quality hold pending inspection or testing. Integrates with SAP QM and LabWare LIMS quality disposition workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; ISO 9001; GMP; LabWare LIMS

description: Actual quantity of material confirmed as received (goods received) at the destination plant. Used to calculate transfer variances, identify transit losses, and trigger inventory reconciliation. Populated upon goods receipt posting in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-Goods Receipt; SAP EWM

description: SAP plant code identifying the destination plant, distribution center, or external warehouse that will receive the transferred material. Corresponds to the receiving plant in SAP MM stock transfer processing.

Descriptive name of the destination plant or facility receiving the stock transfer. Provides human-readable context for reporting and operational dashboards without requiring a join to the plant master.
SAP storage location code within the receiving plant where the material will be placed upon arrival. Identifies the target warehouse area, tank farm, or storage bin at the destination facility.
Date by which the transferred material is required to arrive at the receiving plant. Used for production planning, supply chain scheduling, and on-time delivery performance measurement.

SAP MM movement type code governing the inventory and accounting postings for this transfer (e.g., 641 for goods issue for STO, 101 for goods receipt). Determines the financial and inventory impact in SAP S/4HANA.
SAP plant code identifying the originating plant or manufacturing facility from which the material is being transferred. Corresponds to the issuing plant in SAP MM stock transfer processing.

Descriptive name of the originating plant or facility issuing the stock transfer. Provides human-readable context for reporting and operational dashboards without requiring a join to the plant master.

SAP special stock type indicator identifying whether the transferred material is standard unrestricted stock or a special stock category such as consignment, project stock, or sales order stock. Affects inventory valuation and availability checking.

Externally-known SAP stock transfer order document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA MM module. Used as the primary business reference across ERP, WMS, and logistics systems for tracking the inter-plant movement.
Ordered quantity of material to be transferred from the sending plant to the receiving plant, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents the planned transfer amount at order creation.

Business reason or justification for initiating the inter-plant stock transfer (e.g., production replenishment, inventory rebalancing, quality rework, customer order fulfillment). Used for operational analytics and cost allocation.

Current lifecycle status of the inter-plant stock transfer order. Tracks progression from creation through in-transit movement to receipt confirmation at the destination plant. Classification of the inter-plant transfer indicating the nature of the movement (e.g., plant-to-plant, plant-to-distribution center, cross-company transfer). Determines the SAP movement type and accounting treatment in FI/CO.

Monetary value of the transferred material quantity based on the standard or moving average price at the sending plant. Used for inter-company or inter-plant inventory valuation and financial settlement in SAP FI/CO. Expressed in the company currency.

description: Mode of transportation used to move the material between plants (e.g., road tanker, rail car, pipeline, barge). Relevant for hazardous material transport compliance under DOT 49 CFR and IATA/IMDG regulations for chemical shipments.

reference: DOT 49 CFR; IMDG Code; GHS; OSHA

Unique surrogate identifier for the storage bin master record in the SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) system. Serves as the primary key for all bin-level inventory tracking and directed putaway/picking operations.
Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Business justification: Hazardous storage rules are defined centrally; link bin to rule and remove duplicated class info.
Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Storage bin belongs to a warehouse location; replace free‑text warehouse_number with FK for proper hierarchy.
Aisle identifier within the warehouse where the storage bin is physically located. Used for warehouse navigation, slotting optimization, and pick path sequencing.

Comma-separated list of SAP material type codes (e.g., ROH for raw materials, FERT for finished goods, HALB for semi-finished, VERP for packaging) permitted to be stored in this bin. Enforces material segregation rules for chemical compatibility and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP EWM; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119

description: Type of material handling equipment required to access this storage bin. Determines equipment assignment during transfer order creation and supports warehouse resource planning. Critical for safety compliance in chemical warehouses where certain hazmat bins require specialized handling equipment.

Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the physical storage bin within a warehouse number and storage type. Corresponds to the SAP EWM Lgpla field. Used in directed putaway, picking, and bin-level inventory queries.

Free-text description of the storage bin providing additional context about its physical characteristics, special use restrictions, or operational notes. May include information about dedicated chemical families, special handling requirements, or historical usage notes.

Human-readable descriptive name or label for the storage bin, used in warehouse operations, signage, and reporting. May include aisle, row, and level descriptors (e.g., 'Aisle-A Row-03 Level-02') to aid physical navigation.

Current operational lifecycle status of the storage bin. Active bins are available for putaway and picking. Blocked bins are temporarily unavailable. Quarantine status is applied when contents are under quality hold per QM procedures. Maintenance status indicates the bin is undergoing physical inspection or repair.

Classification of the physical bin configuration. Fixed bins are dedicated to specific materials (e.g., hazardous chemicals); random bins accept any compatible material; bulk bins hold large quantities; tank bins represent liquid storage vessels in tank farms; floor bins are ground-level pallet positions; rack bins are elevated rack locations.

Type of secondary containment infrastructure present at this storage bin to prevent chemical spills from reaching drains or the environment. Secondary bund and tank bund are used for bulk liquid chemical storage; spill pallets for drum storage; lined floor for general chemical areas. Required for EPA SPCC compliance and ISO 14001 environmental management.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 112 (SPCC); ISO 14001; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119

description: Timestamp when the storage bin master record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for bin lifecycle management and supports data governance requirements.
Current number of handling units (pallets, drums, IBCs) physically present in the bin. Reflects the real-time stock count as maintained by SAP EWM goods movements.
Current total volume in cubic meters occupied by stock in the bin. Compared against max_volume_m3 to determine available capacity for putaway decisions.

Current total gross weight in kilograms of all stock currently stored in the bin. Updated upon each goods movement. Used to monitor bin load against maximum weight capacity and support structural safety compliance.

Date on which the storage bin was or is scheduled to be deactivated and removed from active warehouse operations. Null for currently active bins. Supports bin lifecycle management and warehouse restructuring projects.

NFPA fire protection zone or compartment designation assigned to the storage bin. Used to enforce fire load limits, sprinkler system coverage mapping, and emergency response planning for flammable and combustible chemical storage areas.

Indicates whether this bin is a fixed bin permanently assigned to a specific material or material group, as opposed to a random bin available for any compatible material. True = fixed bin assignment; False = random bin. Drives SAP EWM putaway strategy determination.

Indicates whether this storage bin has been formally approved and certified for the storage of hazardous materials in compliance with OSHA PSM, EPA RMP, and GHS regulations. True = approved for hazmat storage; False = not approved.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119; EPA 40 CFR Part 68; GHS

description: Indicates whether this bin is designated as a quarantine storage location for materials under quality hold, out-of-specification (OOS) status, or pending Certificate of Analysis (COA) release. True = quarantine bin; False = standard bin. Integrates with SAP QM quality inspection workflows.

Date of the most recent physical safety or structural inspection of the storage bin, including rack integrity checks, containment integrity, and hazmat compliance verification. Supports OSHA PSM and ISO 45001 occupational health and safety compliance.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to the storage bin master record. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail purposes in compliance with ISO 9001 document control requirements.

Date on which the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count was performed for this storage bin. Used to track inventory accuracy compliance, schedule future counts, and support SOX financial reporting requirements for inventory valuation.
Maximum number of handling units (pallets, drums, IBCs, or containers) that can be stored in the bin simultaneously. Enforced during directed putaway to prevent overfilling.

Maximum allowable stacking height in meters for materials stored in this bin. Enforced to prevent structural overload and comply with OSHA stacking safety regulations. Particularly relevant for drum and IBC storage of liquid chemicals.

Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this bin. Prevents thermal degradation, polymerization, or hazardous decomposition of stored chemicals. Critical for compliance with SDS storage requirements.
Maximum storage volume in cubic meters available within the bin. Used for volumetric capacity planning, putaway feasibility checks, and tank farm liquid volume management.

Maximum allowable gross weight in kilograms that the storage bin can safely hold, including packaging and handling units. Critical for structural safety compliance and overload prevention in chemical warehouses storing dense raw materials or bulk liquids.

Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this bin. Enforced for temperature-sensitive chemical raw materials, intermediates, and finished goods to prevent degradation, crystallization, or phase separation.

Scheduled date for the next mandatory safety or structural inspection of the storage bin. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling and ensures continuous compliance with OSHA, NFPA, and ISO 45001 inspection frequency requirements.

Real-time occupancy state of the storage bin indicating whether the bin is empty, fully occupied, partially occupied, or reserved for an incoming transfer order. Drives directed putaway logic in SAP EWM and supports inventory accuracy reporting.

SAP EWM picking point bin code associated with this storage bin. For bulk or reserve storage bins, identifies the forward pick face bin from which order picking is executed. Supports two-step picking workflows common in chemical distribution operations.

SAP EWM putaway strategy assigned to this bin that determines how incoming stock is directed to storage locations. Fixed strategy assigns stock to pre-defined bins; open storage allows any compatible bin; bulk strategy consolidates large quantities; near-picking places fast-movers close to pick areas; addition-to-existing consolidates partial bins.

Inventory lot rotation strategy applied to this bin for picking sequence determination. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for most chemicals; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is mandatory for materials with expiration dates to prevent use of expired lots; LIFO and LEFO are used in specific bulk storage scenarios. Aligns with GMP and ISO 9001 inventory management requirements.
Row number within the aisle that identifies the horizontal position of the storage bin. Combined with aisle and level, provides the full physical coordinate of the bin within the warehouse.

Vertical level or tier of the storage bin within a rack structure. Level 1 is ground floor; higher numbers indicate elevated rack positions. Used for equipment assignment (e.g., forklift vs. reach truck) and weight distribution planning.

SAP EWM storage section (Lgber) code that subdivides a storage type into logical groupings, such as fast-moving, slow-moving, or temperature-controlled sections. Used to optimize putaway strategies for chemical raw materials and finished goods.

SAP EWM storage type (Lgtyp) code that classifies the physical area of the warehouse where this bin resides, such as high-rack storage, bulk storage, tank farm, or hazardous material area. Drives putaway and picking strategies.

Temperature-controlled storage zone classification for the bin. Ambient bins operate at room temperature; refrigerated bins maintain 2–8°C for temperature-sensitive chemicals or intermediates; heated bins prevent solidification of high-melting-point materials; controlled bins maintain a specific validated temperature range per product specification.

Indicates whether this storage bin requires dedicated ventilation or is located in a ventilated area to manage volatile organic compound (VOC) emissions or hazardous air pollutant (HAP) concentrations. True = ventilation required; False = no special ventilation needed. Supports EHS compliance with EPA and OSHA air quality standards.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 63; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.94; TSCA

Reference to the warehouse operator or forklift driver assigned to execute this transfer order. Used for labor tracking and accountability.

Date and time when the operator physically began executing the transfer order (e.g., first scan of source bin or handling unit). Enables measurement of queue wait time and operator response time.

Free-text or coded reason for cancellation of the transfer order (e.g., material not found at source bin, bin blocked, production order cancelled, duplicate order). Supports root cause analysis and process improvement.
Date and time when the transfer order was cancelled in SAP EWM. Populated only when transfer_order_status = 'cancelled'. Used for exception reporting and warehouse efficiency analysis.

Method used by the operator or system to confirm execution of the transfer order. 'rf_scan' = RF barcode/RFID scanner; 'manual' = paper-based or keyboard entry; 'automated_conveyor' = conveyor system auto-confirmation; 'voice_picking' = voice-directed warehouse system; 'agv' = automated guided vehicle.

Actual quantity confirmed as physically moved by the operator upon transfer order completion. May differ from transfer_quantity due to partial picks, short picks, or discrepancies discovered during execution.
Date and time when the transfer order was fully confirmed in SAP EWM, triggering the inventory goods movement posting. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.
reference: SAP EWM Transfer Order Confirmation; SAP MM Goods Movement

description: Date and time when the transfer order was first created in SAP EWM. Represents the record audit creation time and the start of the transfer order lifecycle.

description: Alphanumeric identifier of the specific storage bin, rack location, or tank to which the material is being moved. Represents the physical destination within the warehouse complex.
SAP EWM storage section within the destination storage type, providing logical grouping of target bins.

SAP EWM storage type code for the target location. Used to validate compatibility with material hazard classification and storage requirements (e.g., flammable, corrosive, oxidizer segregation per OSHA PSM and NFPA codes).

Operational system of record from which this transfer order record was extracted. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and distinguishes between SAP EWM (Extended Warehouse Management), legacy SAP WM, or manually created records.

Planned quantity of material to be moved as specified on the transfer order at creation time. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material (e.g., KG, L, EA, MT).

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Trigger Flag
description: Indicates whether this catalog code automatically triggers a CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) workflow for quality management and continuous improvement. Aligns with GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) and ISO 9001 requirements.

The unique alphanumeric code within the catalog type and code group. This is the actual code value used in PM (Plant Maintenance) notifications and work orders for structured failure analysis. Example: CORR for corrosion, WEAR for wear, FRAC for fracture.
Current lifecycle status of the catalog code. Active codes are available for use in maintenance transactions. Deprecated codes are retained for historical reporting but not available for new entries.

The type of catalog this code belongs to: damage codes (what failed), cause codes (why it failed), activity codes (what was done), object part codes (which component), or symptom codes (how failure manifested). Aligns with SAP PM catalog profile structure.

Hierarchical grouping identifier for the catalog code. Enables multi-level classification and rollup reporting. For example, mechanical failures, electrical failures, instrumentation failures. Corresponds to SAP PM code group.
Detailed description of the catalog code providing full definition, usage guidance, and examples. Used for training and reference documentation.
Brief description of the catalog code, typically 20-40 characters. Used in dropdown lists and compact displays in maintenance transactions.
Date and time when this catalog code entry was created in the system. Used for audit trail and version control.
The date after which this catalog code is no longer valid for new transactions. Null for codes with no planned end date. Historical transactions retain the code for reporting continuity.
The date from which this catalog code becomes valid and available for use in maintenance transactions. Supports versioned catalog management.

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Reportable Flag
description: Indicates whether failures coded with this catalog entry require EHS (Environment Health and Safety) incident reporting to regulatory authorities (EPA, OSHA, ECHA).

The equipment class or category this catalog code is applicable to (e.g., rotating equipment, static equipment, instrumentation, electrical, piping). Enables filtering of relevant codes by equipment type.
Typical equipment downtime duration in hours associated with this failure mode or activity code. Used for production impact planning and scheduling.
Typical repair cost in base currency associated with this failure mode or activity code. Used for maintenance budgeting and cost analysis.
The depth level in the catalog hierarchy. Level 1 is top-level, level 2 is sub-category, etc. Used for reporting aggregation and navigation.

Cross-reference to ISO 14224 (Petroleum, petrochemical and natural gas industries - Collection and exchange of reliability and maintenance data for equipment) failure mode taxonomy. Enables benchmarking against industry standards.
Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code for the catalog code text. Supports multi-language catalog maintenance for global operations.
The most recent date this catalog code was used in a maintenance transaction. Used to identify obsolete codes for catalog cleanup.
Indicates whether this failure mode or activity code automatically triggers a MOC (Management of Change) workflow for process equipment changes. Required for PSM mechanical integrity compliance.
User ID of the person who last modified this catalog code entry. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Date and time when this catalog code entry was last modified. Used for audit trail and version control.
Reference to the parent code group in the hierarchical catalog structure. Enables multi-level rollup and drill-down analysis. Null for top-level groups.
The plant section or process area this catalog code is primarily used in (e.g., reactor area, distillation, packaging, utilities). Supports location-specific failure analysis.
Default priority level associated with this catalog code. Used to auto-populate priority on PM notifications when this code is selected. Critical codes trigger immediate response.

Identifier of the production order that was interrupted by this downtime event. Links to manufacturing order for impact analysis.

business_glossary_term: CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this downtime event requires formal CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) investigation and documentation per quality management system.
Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context about the downtime event not captured in structured fields.
User ID or name of the person who created this downtime event record. Supports accountability and audit trail.
Timestamp when this downtime event record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
Identifier of the DCS or SCADA alarm that triggered or was associated with the downtime event. Links to process control system alarm history.

High-level classification of the downtime event type. Used for OEE availability loss categorization and root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical_failure|electrical_failure|process_upset|material_shortage|changeover|planned_maintenance|utility_failure|operator_shortage|quality_issue|safety_incident — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Total duration of the downtime event in minutes. Calculated as the difference between end and start timestamps. Used directly in OEE availability calculation.
Precise date and time when the equipment was restored to operational status and production resumed.
Business-readable unique identifier for the downtime event, typically generated by MES or DCS system.
Precise date and time when the downtime event began. Captured from DCS/SCADA system or manually recorded by operator.
Current lifecycle status of the downtime event record. Tracks progression from detection through resolution and closure.
Detailed subcategory or specific failure mode within the downtime category. Provides granular classification for maintenance and continuous improvement analysis.
Indicates whether the downtime event resulted in an environmental release or incident requiring EPA or regulatory reporting.
Theoretical minimum time to produce one unit of product under ideal conditions. Used for OEE performance calculation.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this downtime event record. Supports change tracking and accountability.
Timestamp when this downtime event record was last updated. Tracks record change history for audit and compliance.
Unique transaction identifier from the MES system that recorded the downtime event. Supports integration and data lineage.
Reference number of the Management of Change record if the downtime was related to a recent process or equipment modification.

business_glossary_term: MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) Inclusion Flag
description: Indicates whether this downtime event should be counted as a failure event in MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) calculation for reliability analysis.

business_glossary_term: MTTR (Mean Time To Repair) Inclusion Flag
description: Indicates whether this downtime event should be included in MTTR (Mean Time To Repair) calculation for maintainability analysis.

business_glossary_term: OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) Availability Impact Flag
description: Indicates whether this downtime event should be included in the OEE availability component calculation (True) or excluded (False).

business_glossary_term: OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) Performance Impact Flag
description: Indicates whether this downtime event impacted the OEE performance component (speed losses) in addition to availability.

business_glossary_term: OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) Quality Impact Flag
description: Indicates whether this downtime event resulted in quality losses or defects that impact the OEE quality component.
Indicates whether the downtime was scheduled and planned (True) or unplanned/unscheduled (False). Critical for distinguishing availability losses in OEE calculation.
Total planned production time for the period in which the downtime occurred. Used as the denominator in OEE availability calculation.
Number of production batches delayed or rescheduled as a result of this downtime event. Measures schedule adherence impact.
Quantity of product that could not be produced due to the downtime event, measured in the product's base unit of measure. Used for throughput loss calculation.
Unit of measure for the quantity lost (e.g., kg, liters, tons, units). Aligns with material master UOM.
Standardized code representing the type of recovery action performed (e.g., repair, replacement, adjustment, restart).
Detailed description of the corrective actions taken to restore equipment to operational status and resume production.
Standardized code representing the root cause category from the plant's failure mode taxonomy. Enables systematic analysis and trending.
Detailed narrative description of the root cause identified through RCI (Root Cause Investigation) or failure analysis. Supports CAPA and continuous improvement.
Indicates whether the downtime event was associated with a safety incident requiring EHS reporting or investigation.
