Current capacity utilization rate expressed as a percentage, indicating how much of available capacity is being used.
Indicates whether this work center is equipped with CIP (Clean In Place) automated cleaning systems for sanitation between production runs.
Date when this work center was commissioned and put into operational service.
Timestamp when this work center master data record was first created in the system.
Standard number of operators or crew members required to operate this work center during normal production.
Identifier for the DCS (Distributed Control System) node or controller managing this work center. Typically references Honeywell Experion or Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7 system nodes.
Type or vendor of the DCS system controlling this work center for process automation and monitoring.
Standard efficiency factor applied to capacity calculations, representing the expected performance level relative to theoretical maximum.

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Permit Required Flag
description: Indicates whether EHS (Environment Health and Safety) permits or special authorizations are required to operate this work center.
reference: OSHA Process Safety Management, EPA Operating Permits

description: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) classification level for this work center, indicating compliance requirements for pharmaceutical or food-contact chemical production.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211 GMP, ISO 22000 Food Safety

description: Electrical area classification indicating the level of explosion or fire hazard at this work center location per NFPA 70 (National Electrical Code).
reference: NFPA 70 National Electrical Code, IEC 60079 Explosive Atmospheres

description: Date of the most recent major overhaul or significant maintenance activity performed on this work center.
Timestamp when this work center master data record was last updated or modified.
Maximum production batch size that can be processed at this work center, expressed in the standard unit of measure.
Minimum production batch size that can be processed at this work center, expressed in the standard unit of measure.

business_glossary_term: Next Process Safety Review (PSR) Due Date
description: Scheduled date for the next required Process Safety Review or PHA (Process Hazard Analysis) for this work center.
Date of the next scheduled preventive maintenance activity for this work center.
Target OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) percentage for this work center, combining availability, performance, and quality metrics.
Current operational state of the work center indicating availability for production scheduling and execution.

business_glossary_term: Potential of Hydrogen (pH) Control Capability Flag
description: Indicates whether this work center has automated pH (Potential of Hydrogen) control capabilities for chemical process management.
Aveva PI System tag prefix used for real-time process data historian integration and time-series data collection from this work center.
Identifier for the PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) managing discrete control functions for this work center.
Indicates whether this work center has automated pressure control capabilities for process management.
Date of the most recent Process Safety Review or Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) conducted for this work center.

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Node ID
description: SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system node identifier for real-time monitoring and data collection from this work center.
Standard setup or changeover time in minutes required to prepare this work center for a new production run or product.
Standard teardown or cleanup time in minutes required after completing a production run at this work center.
Indicates whether this work center has automated temperature control capabilities for process management.
Operational category indicating the processing mode and operational characteristics of the work center.
Business identifier code for the work center used in production planning and scheduling systems. Typically sourced from SAP PP (Production Planning) module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Master Data Standards

description: Descriptive name of the work center for human identification and reporting purposes.
Classification of the work center based on its primary production function within the chemical manufacturing process.
Unique identifier for the batch genealogy record tracking parent-child lineage relationships between chemical batches.
The identifier of the production operator who executed or confirmed the batch transfer creating this genealogy relationship.

Foreign key linking to production.manufacturing_order. Business justification: Link batch genealogy to its manufacturing order for traceability; production_order_number is redundant once the FK exists.
Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Batch Genealogy traceability links each child batch to the originating raw material for audit and regulatory reporting.
The BOM item number from the production recipe that defines this parent-child material relationship. Links to SAP PP BOM structure.
The batch number of the downstream child batch (intermediate or finished good) produced in this production relationship. Part of the lot genealogy traceability chain.
The COA reference number associated with this batch genealogy relationship, linking to quality test results and compliance documentation.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, GMP Good Manufacturing Practice

description: The percentage of the parent batch consumed to produce the child batch, enabling yield and material balance calculations.
The timestamp when this batch genealogy record was first created in the system, providing audit trail for data lineage.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format, SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act

description: The customer order number if this batch genealogy relationship was created for a specific customer order, enabling customer-specific traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales and Distribution

description: The batch identifier from the DCS (Honeywell Experion or Siemens PCS 7) that recorded this batch relationship in real-time process control.
reference: Honeywell Experion DCS, Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7

description: The deviation number if this batch relationship occurred under non-standard conditions requiring documentation and approval.
The expiration date of the child batch produced in this genealogy relationship, inherited or calculated from parent batch shelf life.
reference: GMP Good Manufacturing Practice, FEFO First Expired First Out

description: The current status of this batch genealogy record: active (normal traceability), archived (historical record), quarantined (batch under quality hold), recalled (batch subject to recall action).
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, FDA Recall Regulations

description: The timestamp when this batch genealogy record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format, SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act

description: The MES transaction identifier that recorded this batch genealogy event, providing audit trail linkage to the execution system.
reference: MES Manufacturing Execution System, ISA-95 Manufacturing Operations

description: The MOC number if this batch genealogy relationship was created under a process change or deviation. Links to EHS Management of Change records.
reference: PSM Process Safety Management, OSHA Process Safety Management

description: The batch number of the upstream parent batch (raw material or intermediate) consumed in this production relationship. Part of the lot genealogy traceability chain.
The manufacturing plant code where this batch genealogy relationship was created. Links to SAP organizational structure.

The specific process step or operation code in the manufacturing route where this batch relationship occurred (e.g., synthesis, blending, filtration, drying). Maps to MES (Manufacturing Execution System) operation codes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Planning, MES Manufacturing Execution System

description: Human-readable description of the process step where the batch relationship occurred, providing context for the genealogy link.
The quality status of the batch relationship at the time of transfer: released (approved for use), blocked (quality hold), restricted (conditional use), inspection (pending QC).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Quality Management, ISO 9001

description: The quantity of material transferred from parent batch to child batch in this genealogy relationship. Measured in the unit of measure specified.

Boolean flag indicating whether this batch genealogy relationship meets all applicable regulatory traceability requirements (REACH, FDA, TSCA). True if compliant, False if non-compliant or pending verification.
reference: REACH Regulation, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, TSCA Toxic Substances Control Act

description: The type of genealogy relationship between parent and child batches: consumed (parent used to make child), produced (child created from parent), split (one parent divided into multiple children), merged (multiple parents combined into one child), blended (multiple parents mixed into child), reworked (defective batch reprocessed).
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice)

description: Free-text remarks or notes about this batch genealogy relationship, capturing any special conditions, observations, or contextual information.

The source system that created this batch genealogy record: SAP_PP (SAP Production Planning), MES (Manufacturing Execution System), DCS (Distributed Control System), LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System), MANUAL (manual entry).
reference: SAP S/4HANA, MES, Honeywell Experion DCS, Siemens PCS 7, LabWare LIMS

description: The storage location code where the batch transfer occurred or where the child batch was placed after production.
The depth level of this batch relationship in the overall genealogy chain, where 1 represents direct parent-child, 2 represents grandparent-grandchild, etc. Enables multi-level traceability queries.
The direction of traceability this record supports: forward (parent to child), backward (child to parent), or bidirectional (both directions).
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, FDA 21 CFR Part 11

description: The date when the material transfer from parent to child batch occurred, establishing the temporal sequence in the genealogy chain.
The precise timestamp when the material transfer from parent to child batch was recorded in the MES or DCS system, providing exact traceability timing.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format, MES Manufacturing Execution System

description: The unit of measure for the quantity transferred: KG (kilogram), L (liter), MT (metric ton), GAL (gallon), LB (pound), TON (ton), M3 (cubic meter). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|L|MT|GAL|LB|TON|M3 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The specific work center or production line where the batch relationship occurred. Maps to SAP PP work center master data.
The yield percentage achieved in this batch transformation from parent to child, calculated as (actual output / theoretical output) * 100.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, SPC Statistical Process Control

description: Unique identifier for the electronic batch record. Primary key for the batch record entity. Serves as the authoritative reference for all batch-level traceability, genealogy, and regulatory audit requirements.

Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Batch record must reference the specific chemical product to associate batch data with product specifications and compliance documents.

Foreign key linking to customer.site. Business justification: Needed for logistics and traceability: batch records must reference the customer site where the product will be delivered, supporting shipping plans, regulatory SDS/COA delivery, and site‑level yield
Primary reactor, vessel, or processing unit used for this batch. Links to the equipment master for asset tracking and maintenance correlation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM (Plant Maintenance); ISO 55001 (Asset Management)

description: Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formulation. Business justification: Ensures batch traceability to the exact experimental formulation developed in R&D, needed for quality audits and compliance reporting.
Reference to the master formula or recipe used for this batch. Captures the formulation version and Bill of Materials (BOM) applied during production.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP (Recipe Management); GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice)

description: Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Batch records must capture the exact formula version used for regulatory traceability and deviation analysis.

FK to quality.inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id — Batch-to-QC traceability is mandatory for cGMP compliance. Every batch record must link to its quality inspection lot for release decisions. Without this, cannot answer 'what QC results exist for batc

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Needed for Batch‑Level Expense Tracking: batches are billed against internal orders to capture project‑specific production costs.
Foreign key linking to order.line_item. Business justification: Batch‑to‑order line mapping enables batch traceability, invoicing, and regulatory batch‑lot reporting for each sold line item.
Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Enables batch traceability required by FDA/EMA regulations linking batch records to physical lots.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.notification. Business justification: Needed for Batch Execution Log to capture the maintenance notification that caused a batch hold, used in compliance and downtime analysis reports.
Reference to the production order that authorized this batch. Links the batch record to the planning and scheduling system (SAP PP Production Planning).
Foreign key linking to planning.mrp_run. Business justification: Each batch record originates from an MRP run; linking supports the Batch‑to‑MRP traceability audit.
Reference to the primary operator or production technician responsible for executing this batch. Used for accountability, training tracking, and electronic signature compliance (21 CFR Part 11).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); ISO 9001

description: Reference to the authorized person (typically QA manager or production manager) who gave final approval for batch release. Required for GMP batch release authorization.
Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: BATCH: Each batch fulfills a specific quote line; FK supports batch‑level traceability to sales commitments and compliance reporting.
Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: REQUIRED: Quality and traceability reports link each batch record to the goods receipt that delivered the raw material lot.
Reference to the quality assurance (QA) or quality control (QC) personnel who reviewed this batch record. Required for GMP compliance and electronic signature audit trail.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); 21 CFR Part 11; QA (Quality Assurance)

description: Actual quantity produced by this batch after completion. Used to calculate yield percentage, material efficiency, and production performance (OEE - Overall Equipment Effectiveness).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; ISO 22400 (Key Performance Indicators for Manufacturing)

description: Unit of measure for the actual yield quantity. Should match batch_size_uom for yield percentage calculation.
reference: ISO 80000 (Quantities and Units); SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Date and time when the batch record received final approval for release to inventory. Part of the electronic signature audit trail and batch release documentation.

Externally-known unique batch identifier (LOT number) used for product traceability, customer communication, Certificate of Analysis (COA) issuance, and regulatory reporting. Must be globally unique across all production history.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ISO 9001; GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice)

description: Planned or target quantity for this batch expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for yield calculation, material consumption planning, and production scheduling.
Unit of measure for the batch size quantity (e.g., KG, L, MT, GAL). Must align with the formula base unit and inventory unit of measure.
reference: ISO 80000 (Quantities and Units); SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Current lifecycle state of the batch in the manufacturing execution workflow. Governs whether the batch can be released to inventory, requires quality review, or is blocked for regulatory or quality reasons.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); ISO 9001; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Boolean indicator that a Certificate of Analysis (COA) has been issued for this batch. COA is required for customer shipments and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 9001; GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); COA (Certificate of Analysis)

description: Unique identifier for the Certificate of Analysis issued for this batch. Links the batch record to the quality test results and customer documentation.
Free-text field for operator or supervisor comments, observations, or notes about the batch execution. Used for capturing contextual information not covered by structured fields.
Date and time when this batch record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for electronic records compliance (21 CFR Part 11).

Unique batch identifier from the Distributed Control System (Honeywell Experion, Siemens PCS 7) that executed the process automation. Links the batch record to real-time process data historian (Aveva PI System).
reference: Honeywell Experion DCS; Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7; Aveva PI System

description: Number of process deviations, exceptions, or Out of Specification (OOS) events recorded during batch execution. Triggers CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) and RCI (Root Cause Investigation) workflows.
reference: CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action); GMP; ISO 9001

description: Boolean indicator that one or more exceptions occurred during batch execution. Used to filter batches requiring quality review or management attention.

Date after which the batch is no longer suitable for use or sale. Calculated based on manufacturing date and product shelf-life specification. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); ISO 9001; FEFO Inventory Practice

description: Version identifier of the formula or recipe used for this batch. Critical for traceability when formulas are revised due to MOC (Management of Change) or process optimization.
reference: MOC (Management of Change); ISO 9001; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Boolean indicator that this batch was produced under GMP-compliant conditions and meets all regulatory requirements for pharmaceutical or food-contact chemical production.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); FDA (Food and Drug Administration)

description: Date and time when this batch record was last updated. Part of the audit trail for electronic records compliance and change tracking.
Calendar date of batch production. Used for shelf-life calculation, expiry date determination, and regulatory labeling (GHS - Globally Harmonized System).
Date and time when batch production was completed and the batch was ready for quality inspection. Used for cycle time analysis and production scheduling.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SAP S/4HANA PP; Honeywell Experion DCS

description: Date and time when batch production was initiated on the manufacturing floor. Captured from MES (Manufacturing Execution System) or DCS (Distributed Control System) transaction log.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SAP S/4HANA PP; Honeywell Experion DCS

description: Version of the MES or DCS system that recorded this batch. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and system validation audit trail.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); MES (Manufacturing Execution System)

description: Unique transaction identifier from the MES system that initiated or recorded this batch. Used for audit trail linkage and electronic records compliance (21 CFR Part 11).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); MES (Manufacturing Execution System)

description: Average pH level during the critical reaction phase. Key process parameter for chemical synthesis quality control and product specification compliance.
reference: SPC (Statistical Process Control); ISO 9001; pH (Potential of Hydrogen)

description: Average process pressure during the critical reaction phase, measured in bar. Key process parameter for quality control and process safety management (PSM).
reference: PSM (Process Safety Management); SPC; Honeywell Experion DCS

description: Average process temperature during the critical reaction phase, measured in degrees Celsius. Key process parameter for quality control and SPC (Statistical Process Control) analysis.
reference: SPC (Statistical Process Control); ISO 9001; Honeywell Experion DCS

description: Total duration of the chemical reaction phase in minutes. Critical process parameter for yield optimization and product quality consistency.
Comma-separated list or summary of recipe phases executed during this batch (e.g., charging, heating, reaction, cooling, discharge). Provides high-level execution summary for batch genealogy.
reference: ISA-88 (Batch Control Standard); SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Boolean indicator that this batch is under regulatory hold and cannot be released to inventory or shipped until regulatory clearance is obtained. May be set by EHS (Environment Health and Safety) or quality assurance.
reference: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency); OSHA; EHS (Environment Health and Safety)

description: Date when the batch must be retested to confirm continued compliance with quality specifications. Common for raw materials and intermediate products with extended storage periods.
Current status of the batch record review and approval workflow. Governs whether the batch can be released to inventory or requires additional quality or management review.
reference: GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice); ISO 9001; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date and time when the batch record was reviewed and approved or rejected by quality assurance. Part of the electronic signature audit trail (21 CFR Part 11).
Calculated yield efficiency as a percentage of actual yield to planned batch size. Key performance indicator for production efficiency and process optimization (SPC - Statistical Process Control).
reference: ISO 22400 (KPI for Manufacturing); SPC (Statistical Process Control)

description: Unique identifier for the BOM consumption transaction record.
Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: BOM consumption tracking needs product master reference to validate material codes, safety data, and costing.
User ID of the person who posted the consumption transaction in the system.
Reference to the production order against which this material consumption was posted.
Reference to the raw material or intermediate component consumed in this transaction.
Actual quantity of material consumed and posted against the production order.
Flag indicating whether this consumption was posted automatically via backflushing at production confirmation.

business_glossary_term: Bill of Materials (BOM) Line Item Number
description: Line item sequence number in the BOM structure indicating which component this consumption relates to.
CAS registry number of the consumed chemical substance for regulatory tracking and compliance.
Cost center to which the material consumption cost is allocated.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts.
Date of the physical goods movement or consumption event.
Name or identifier of the person or entity receiving the goods for production use.
Date and time when this consumption record was last updated or modified.
Lot or batch number of the consumed material, enabling full traceability and genealogy.
Unique document number generated by SAP MM for the goods movement transaction.
Fiscal year of the material document for archival and retrieval purposes.
SAP MM movement type code indicating the nature of the goods movement (e.g., 261 for goods issue to production order, 262 for reversal).
Routing operation number to which this material consumption is allocated.
Quantity of material planned to be consumed according to the BOM for this production order.
Manufacturing plant or facility code where the consumption occurred.
Date on which the material consumption was posted in the system.
Exact date and time when the consumption record was created in the system.
Business identifier of the production order for which materials were consumed.
Code indicating the reason for consumption variance or manual posting, if applicable.
Line item number within the reservation document.
Material reservation number linked to this consumption, if applicable.
Flag indicating whether this consumption record is a reversal of a previous posting.
Material document number of the original transaction that this record reverses, if applicable.
Code indicating special stock type (e.g., consignment, project stock, sales order stock).
Standard cost per unit of measure for the consumed material at the time of posting.
Code identifying the storage location from which the material was withdrawn.
Total cost impact of this consumption transaction, calculated as actual_quantity_consumed * standard_cost_per_unit.
Unit of measure for the consumed quantity (e.g., KG, L, MT, GAL).
Physical location or point where the material was delivered for consumption.
Valuation type or split valuation category for the consumed material.
Percentage variance between planned and actual consumption, calculated as (variance_quantity / planned_quantity) * 100.
Difference between planned and actual consumed quantity (actual minus planned).
Code of the work center where the material was consumed during production.
Primary key for control_recipe
Identifier of the catalyst used in this recipe.
Self-referencing FK on control_recipe (template_control_recipe_id)
Current approval state of the recipe.
Timestamp when the recipe was formally approved for production.
Business reason for the most recent change to the recipe.
Regulatory framework governing the recipe.
Timestamp when the recipe record was first created in the system.
Free‑form description of the recipe purpose, scope, and key characteristics.
Date after which the recipe is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).
Date from which the recipe is valid for production.
Logical grouping of equipment (e.g., reactor, distillation column) that the recipe applies to.
Desired liquid flow rate in liters per hour.
Indicates whether this recipe is the default for its product line.
Timestamp of the most recent production run that used this recipe.
Organizational department responsible for the recipe.
Target pH value for the reaction environment.
Target pressure in bar for the process step.
External code or catalogue number that uniquely identifies the recipe across systems.
Human‑readable name of the control recipe used by operators and engineers.
Classification of the recipe based on production mode.
Internal revision counter for change management.
Safety classification based on hazard potential.
Current lifecycle status of the recipe.
Desired product yield expressed as a percentage for this recipe.
Target temperature in degrees Celsius for the process step defined by the recipe.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the recipe record.
Number of times the recipe has been executed.
Free‑form comment describing the changes in this version.
Sequential version number of the recipe definition.

business_glossary_term: Distributed Control System (DCS) Event Log ID
description: Unique identifier for each DCS/SCADA event log entry. Primary key for the event log. Inferred role: EVENT_LOG.

Unique identifier of the operator who acknowledged the event, performed the action, or authored the shift entry. Links to workforce/employee master data. Required for audit trail and accountability per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records, OSHA PSM, ISO 9001

description: Identifier of the manufacturing order or production batch active at the time of the event. Links event to specific production run for lot genealogy and batch record traceability. Maps to SAP PP Production Order.
reference: ISA-95 Enterprise-Control System Integration, 21 CFR Part 11

description: Foreign key linking to research.research_process_simulation. Business justification: Connects DCS events to the HYSYS simulation model used for process design, enabling validation of control actions against the simulated baseline.

Indicates whether the event (typically an alarm or interlock) has been acknowledged by an operator. True if acknowledged, False if unacknowledged. Critical for alarm management and operator response tracking.
Date and time when the event was acknowledged by an operator. Null if not yet acknowledged. Used to calculate alarm response time and operator performance metrics. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISA-18.2 Management of Alarm Systems, 21 CFR Part 11

description: Configured alarm threshold value that triggered the alarm for alarm-type events. Null for non-alarm events. Used for alarm rationalization and setpoint optimization.

Current state of the alarm for alarm-type events. Active: alarm condition present. Cleared: alarm condition resolved. Suppressed: alarm temporarily disabled. Shelved: alarm acknowledged but not yet cleared. Null for non-alarm events.
Number of production batches completed during the shift for shift summary events. Null for non-shift-summary events. Key performance indicator for shift productivity.
Number of production batches in progress at shift end for shift summary events. Null for non-shift-summary events. Critical for shift handover continuity.
New control mode after a mode_change event. Null for non-mode-change events. Tracks transitions between automatic, manual, cascade, remote, and local control modes.
Previous control mode before a mode_change event. Null for non-mode-change events. Tracks transitions between automatic, manual, cascade, remote, and local control modes.
Identifier of the DCS/SCADA node or controller that generated the event. Maps to specific Honeywell Experion or Siemens PCS 7 controller instances within the distributed control architecture.
Duration of the event in seconds. For alarms: time from activation to clearance. For interlocks: trip duration. For mode changes: time in transient state. Null for instantaneous events.
Summary of environmental monitoring readings during the shift (e.g., emissions, VOC levels, wastewater parameters). Null if not recorded. Supports EPA and environmental compliance reporting.
reference: EPA Environmental Protection Agency, ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Free-text description of equipment problems, malfunctions, or maintenance needs identified during the shift. Null if no issues. Used for shift handover and maintenance planning.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, OSHA PSM

description: Detailed narrative description of the event. For alarms: alarm message text. For operator actions: action description. For shift handovers: handover notes and instructions. For shift summaries: comprehensive shift narrative.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, 21 CFR Part 11

description: Precise date and time when the event occurred in the DCS/SCADA system. Critical for chronological event sequencing, alarm analysis, and incident investigation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records

description: Classification of the event type. Discriminates between alarms, interlocks, operator actions, shift documentation, and system state changes. Critical for event filtering, alarm rationalization, and safety analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alarm|interlock_trip|operator_override|setpoint_change|mode_change|shift_handover|shift_summary|shift_observation|system_fault|process_deviation|safety_event — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ISA-18.2 Management of Alarm Systems, OSHA PSM

description: Detailed instructions and action items for the incoming shift. Null for non-handover events. Ensures operational continuity and clear communication between shifts.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, OSHA PSM

description: Unique identifier of the safety interlock that tripped for interlock_trip events. Null for non-interlock events. Links to interlock logic documentation and safety instrumented systems (SIS).
reference: IEC 61511 Functional Safety - Safety Instrumented Systems, OSHA PSM

description: Free-text description of raw material shortages or supply issues encountered during the shift. Null if none. Used for shift handover and supply chain escalation.
Number of operators on duty during the shift for shift-related events. Null for non-shift events. Used for staffing analysis and workload assessment.
reference: OSHA Occupational Safety and Health Administration

description: Priority classification of the event. Critical and high priority events require immediate operator response. Used for alarm rationalization and operator workload analysis per ISA-18.2 guidelines.
reference: ISA-18.2 Management of Alarm Systems, OSHA PSM

description: Actual process measurement value at the time of the event (e.g., temperature, pressure, flow rate, pH). Null if not applicable. Used for alarm analysis and process deviation investigation.
Unit of measure for the process value (e.g., degC, bar, pH, kg/h, L/min, ppm). Null if process_value is null. Critical for engineering analysis.
Free-text description of batches or materials placed on quality hold during the shift. Null if none. Critical for QA/QC tracking and batch disposition.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems, GMP Good Manufacturing Practice

description: Timestamp when this event log record was created in the lakehouse silver layer. Distinct from event_timestamp (when the event occurred in the DCS). Used for data lineage and ETL audit. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Timestamp when this event log record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Free-text description of safety observations, near-misses, or EHS concerns noted during the shift. Null if none. Critical for Process Safety Management and continuous improvement.
reference: OSHA PSM Process Safety Management, OSHA 1910.119

description: Unit of measure for setpoint values (e.g., degC, bar, pH, kg/h, L/min). Null for non-setpoint-change events. Critical for engineering analysis and process control.
New setpoint value after a setpoint_change event. Null for non-setpoint-change events. Used for audit trail and process optimization analysis.
Previous setpoint value before a setpoint_change event. Null for non-setpoint-change events. Used for audit trail and process optimization analysis.
Calendar date of the shift for shift-related events (shift_handover, shift_summary, shift_observation). Null for non-shift events. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Type of shift for shift-related events. Identifies day, afternoon (swing), night, weekend, or holiday shifts. Null for non-shift events. Used for shift handover continuity and staffing analysis.
reference: OSHA Occupational Safety and Health Administration

description: Identifier of the source DCS/SCADA system that generated the event. Distinguishes between Honeywell Experion DCS, Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7, Aveva PI System, and other SCADA platforms.
Name of the shift supervisor on duty for shift-related events. Null for non-shift events. Provides accountability and escalation contact for shift operations.
reference: OSHA PSM Process Safety Management, ISO 9001

description: Unique process tag identifier from the DCS/SCADA system representing the specific instrument, sensor, valve, or control point associated with this event. Used for traceability to P&ID diagrams and process control loops.
reference: ISA-5.1 Instrumentation Symbols and Identification, ISA-95

business_glossary_term: Manufacturing Execution System (MES) Execution Log ID
description: Unique identifier for the MES execution log record. Primary key for the execution log entry capturing a single step or phase execution within a production batch or process order.
Reference to the production batch or lot for which this execution step was performed. Enables lot genealogy and traceability.
Reference to the production equipment or reactor unit on which this operation was performed. Links execution to physical asset.
Reference to the operator or production worker who executed or confirmed this operation step. Critical for accountability and electronic signature compliance.
Reference to the parent process order or production order under which this execution step was performed. Links the execution log to the overall manufacturing order.
Boolean indicator of whether this execution step requires supervisory or quality approval before the batch can proceed. True indicates approval workflow is required.
Date and time when supervisory or quality approval was granted for this execution step. Part of the approval audit trail.
Additional free-text comments or notes entered by the operator or supervisor regarding this execution step. Provides supplementary context for the batch record.
Source from which the execution data was captured (manual operator entry, automatic DCS feed, SCADA system, PLC, LIMS import). Critical for data integrity assessment.

Unique identifier for the process order. Primary key for the process order entity in the SAP PP (Production Planning) module.

description: Identifier linking multiple process orders in a production campaign. Used for extended manufacturing runs of the same product to minimize changeover, reduce CIP cycles, and optimize asset utilization.

Identifier for the control recipe downloaded to the DCS (Honeywell Experion, Siemens PCS 7) or PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) for automated execution. Links master recipe to real-time process control logic.

Foreign key linking to formulation.formula_version. Business justification: Process Order Execution Sheet tracks the specific formula version executed for compliance and quality release.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Required for Production‑Maintenance Coordination Report that matches each process order with its scheduled maintenance work order to avoid line conflicts.
Foreign key linking to rawmaterial.material_master. Business justification: Process Order Execution requires raw‑material safety and quality data; FK enables real‑time compliance checks.

Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: Production scheduling dashboards require linking process orders directly to the originating sales order for capacity planning and KPI tracking.
Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: PROCESS_ORDER: Schedules execution of a quoted line item; linking enables alignment of production planning with sales commitments.
Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Supports Process Order Planned Inventory Allocation used in material planning dashboards.
Actual timestamp when manufacturing execution completed and product was discharged, as confirmed by MES or operator. Triggers quality inspection workflow and lot release process.
Actual timestamp when manufacturing execution began, as confirmed by MES or operator. Used for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation and schedule variance analysis.

Classification of process order scale. Lab for R&D bench-scale (<10kg), pilot for scale-up trials (10-1000kg), commercial for standard production (>1000kg), full_scale for maximum reactor capacity utilization.
Actual quantity of finished product manufactured and confirmed by operator or MES. May differ from planned due to yield loss, reaction efficiency, or quality rejections.
Financial cost center to which process order costs (raw materials, labor, utilities, overhead) are allocated. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation and OpEx (Operating Expenditure) tracking.
Timestamp when the process order record was created in SAP PP. Marks the beginning of the order lifecycle for lead time analysis and planning cycle measurement.
Identifier for the DCS or SCADA system instance managing this process order. Maps to Honeywell Experion, Siemens SIMATIC PCS 7, or Aveva PI System historian for real-time data integration.

EPA or state environmental permit number authorizing manufacture of this chemical. Required for VOC (Volatile Organic Compound), HAP (Hazardous Air Pollutant), and TRI reportable substances. Links to air quality monitoring and emissions reporting.

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) is required from the vendor to address audit findings before or after qualification approval.
reference: ISO 9001 CAPA Process, GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice)

description: Score for logistics and delivery performance including on-time delivery rate, lead time reliability, and order fulfillment accuracy.
Date from which the vendor qualification becomes effective and the vendor is authorized for procurement activities.
Score for environmental, health, and safety compliance assessment including EHS management systems, incident history, and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management, ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: Date when the vendor qualification expires and requires renewal or re-qualification assessment.
Score for financial health assessment including credit rating, financial statements review, and business continuity risk.
Number of critical findings identified during the qualification audit that represent major non-conformances or immediate risks requiring resolution before approval.
Number of major findings identified during the qualification audit that represent significant non-conformances requiring corrective action.
Number of minor findings or observations identified during the qualification audit that represent opportunities for improvement.
Indicates whether the vendor holds valid GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) certification for pharmaceutical or food-contact chemical manufacturing.
Date when the vendor qualification process was formally initiated.
Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification.
Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification.

Primary chemical material category or product family that this qualification covers (e.g., solvents, polymers, catalysts, intermediates, specialty chemicals).
Scheduled date for the next periodic review or re-qualification assessment of the vendor.
Additional notes, comments, or observations related to the vendor qualification assessment, including special conditions or restrictions.
Business identifier for the vendor qualification record. Format: VQ-YYYYNNNN where YYYY is year and NNNN is sequence.
Detailed description of the scope of qualification including product categories, material types, and services covered by this qualification assessment.
Composite numerical score representing the overall qualification assessment result, typically on a 0-100 scale based on weighted criteria.

Current lifecycle status of the vendor qualification: draft (initiated), in_progress (assessment underway), under_review (pending approval), approved (vendor qualified), rejected (vendor not qualified), suspended (temporarily disqualified), expired (qualification period ended). Type of qualification assessment being performed: initial (new vendor), periodic (scheduled review), for-cause (triggered by incident), re-qualification (after suspension), or expedited (fast-track for critical need).
Score for quality management system assessment including QMS (Quality Management System) certification, process controls, and product quality history.

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the vendor is compliant with REACH (Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals) regulation for European chemical substance registration.

Score for regulatory compliance assessment covering REACH (Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals), TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act), GHS (Globally Harmonized System), and other applicable chemical regulations.
Detailed explanation of why the vendor qualification was rejected, including specific deficiencies or non-conformances that led to the rejection decision.
Indicates whether the vendor is a member of the Responsible Care program administered by the American Chemistry Council (ACC) or equivalent regional program.
Overall supply risk classification of the vendor based on criticality of materials, single-source dependency, regulatory complexity, and business impact.
Planned date by which the qualification assessment should be completed and decision rendered.

business_glossary_term: Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the vendor is compliant with TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act) requirements for chemical substances manufactured or imported into the United States.

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliance Status
description: Vendor compliance status with European REACH regulation for chemical substance registration and authorization.

business_glossary_term: Safety Data Sheet (SDS) Compliance Rate Percentage
description: Percentage of shipments accompanied by current, GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheets.

business_glossary_term: Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA) Compliance Status
description: Vendor compliance status with US EPA TSCA requirements for chemical substance inventory and reporting.

description: The specific end-use application or process for which the chemical product has been qualified at the customer site (e.g., automotive coating adhesion, semiconductor etch bath, pharmaceutical excipient, agricultural formulation carrier).

description: Current lifecycle state of the customer product qualification. 'approved' = fully qualified for commercial supply; 'conditionally_approved' = approved with restrictions or deviations; 'pending_review' = under evaluation; 'suspended' = temporarily halted pending investigation; 'revoked' = permanently withdrawn; 'expired' = past expiry date without renewal.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Name or identifier of the customer representative (e.g., quality engineer, procurement manager) who formally approved the qualification. Business-confidential as it identifies a specific customer decision-maker.
The name or employee ID of the internal Chemical Mfg representative (e.g., technical sales, quality manager) who managed and approved the qualification from the supplier side.
The CAS Registry Number of the primary chemical substance in the qualified product. Used for regulatory cross-referencing under REACH, TSCA, and customer EHS systems.
reference: CAS (Chemical Abstracts Service); REACH; TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act); ECHA

description: The minimum number of calendar days advance notice required to be given to the customer before implementing any change to the qualified product. Populated only when change_notification_required_flag is True.

Indicates whether the customer requires formal notification (e.g., PCN - Product Change Notification) before any change to the qualified product formulation, process, or raw material source. Critical in automotive and electronics supply chains.
reference: IATF 16949; JEDEC JESD46 (Product Change Notification); ISO 9001

description: Reference to the COA template configured for this customer-product qualification. Defines which quality parameters and test results are reported on the COA accompanying each shipment.
reference: ISO 9001; LabWare LIMS; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: The timestamp when this qualification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The qualification record identifier in Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer-facing qualification activities. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRM.
The customer's own internal part number or material code assigned to this qualified chemical product. Used for cross-referencing in purchase orders, COA requests, and EDI transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Material Info Record; ANSI X12 EDI

description: The customer's internal specification document number that defines the quality requirements for this qualified product. Referenced on COA and COC documents during shipment.
reference: ISO 9001; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; LabWare LIMS

description: The date from which this qualification record is binding and commercial supply may commence. May differ from qualification_date if there is a ramp-up or transition period.
The date until which this qualification is contractually binding for commercial supply. Null for open-ended qualifications. Used in supply chain planning and order management validation.
Indicates whether Chemical Mfg is the sole qualified supplier for this product at this customer site. True = exclusive supply arrangement; False = customer has multiple qualified suppliers.
The date on which the customer qualification expires and must be renewed or re-qualified. Null indicates an open-ended qualification with no defined expiry. Critical for supply continuity planning.

The end-market industry segment of the customer for which this qualification applies. Drives regulatory requirements, COA content, and qualification rigor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|electronics|pharmaceutical|agriculture|construction|consumer_goods|industrial|aerospace|food_beverage|coatings — promote to reference product]
reference: ACC (American Chemistry Council); ECHA; FDA; IATF 16949

description: The timestamp when this qualification record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Qualification ID
description: The qualification record identifier in LabWare LIMS, used to cross-reference lab trial data, test results, and analytical reports that supported the qualification decision.
The maximum annual or periodic supply volume in kilograms approved under this qualification. Volumes exceeding this threshold require customer notification or re-qualification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; Customer Supply Agreement

description: The minimum annual or periodic supply volume in kilograms approved under this qualification. Volumes below this threshold may require re-qualification or customer notification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; Customer Supply Agreement

description: Free-text field for additional qualification conditions, restrictions, or contextual information not captured in structured fields. Examples: special handling requirements, packaging restrictions, labelling requirements specific to this customer.

The specific chemical grade or quality tier of the product that has been qualified (e.g., Technical Grade, Reagent Grade, Electronic Grade, Pharmaceutical Grade, Food Grade). Critical differentiator in chemical supply.
The date on which the product qualification was formally approved and granted by the customer. This is the effective start date for commercial supply under this qualification.

The method by which the product was qualified for use at the customer site. 'lab_trial' = bench-scale testing at customer or supplier lab; 'plant_trial' = full-scale production trial at customer facility; 'third_party_cert' = qualification via third-party certification body; 'desk_review' = documentation-only review; 'customer_audit' = qualification via customer site audit; 'equivalency_study' = qualification via equivalency to an already-approved product.

Defines the geographic or organizational scope of the qualification. 'site_specific' = applies only to the named customer site; 'global_account' = applies across all sites of the customer account; 'regional' = applies to a defined regional cluster of sites; 'product_family' = applies to a family of related products.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; Salesforce CRM

description: The European Chemicals Agency (ECHA) REACH registration number for the qualified substance. Required for supply into EU markets. Format: 01-XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX-XX-XXXX.
reference: REACH (Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals); ECHA

description: The primary regulatory or quality standard under which this qualification was conducted (e.g., IATF 16949 for automotive, ICH Q7 for pharmaceutical API, IPC-7711 for electronics, ISO 9001 for general industrial). Drives audit and documentation requirements.
reference: ISO 9001; IATF 16949; GMP; FDA; ECHA REACH

description: The number of calendar days before the expiry date that the renewal process must be initiated to ensure supply continuity. Used to trigger renewal workflow alerts in the quality management system.

Indicates whether this qualification requires periodic renewal before the expiry date. True = renewal process must be initiated; False = qualification is perpetual or renewal is not contractually required.
The issue date of the Safety Data Sheet version referenced in this qualification. Used to verify that the customer was provided with current GHS-compliant hazard information at time of qualification.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System); OSHA HazCom 2012; REACH Article 31

description: The version number of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) that was current at the time of qualification. Ensures traceability between the qualification and the hazard communication documentation provided to the customer.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System); OSHA HazCom 2012; REACH Article 31

description: The Stock Keeping Unit code identifying the specific product variant approved for supply. Aligns with SAP material number and customer-facing product catalogue.
Narrative description of any customer-approved deviations from the standard product specification included in this qualification. Populated only when spec_deviation_flag is True.
reference: ISO 9001; CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) Process

description: Indicates whether this qualification includes customer-approved deviations from the standard product specification. True = one or more approved deviations exist; False = qualification is to standard specification.
The time period to which the approved volume range (min/max) applies. Defines whether volume limits are annual, quarterly, monthly, or per-order constraints.
Unique surrogate identifier for the customer regulatory and compliance profile record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Reference to the customer account for whom this regulatory profile applies. Links to the customer master record in Salesforce CRM / SAP S/4HANA SD.

Foreign key linking to inventory.hazmat_storage_rule. Business justification: Ensures Regulatory Compliance Mapping, linking a customer's regulatory profile to the specific hazmat storage rule applied for safe handling and reporting.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory contact person is an employee; linking enables tracking of communications and compliance responsibilities.

Comma-separated list of chemical product categories or families that are approved for sale to this customer under the current regulatory profile (e.g., solvents, adhesives, specialty polymers). Restricts order processing to compliant product types.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Indicates whether the customer has agreed to grant the supplier audit rights to verify compliance with regulatory requirements, safe handling practices, and end-use declarations for supplied chemicals.

description: Date on which the customer formally agreed to audit rights provisions. Used to track agreement currency and renewal obligations.
reference: ISO 9001, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, ACC Responsible Care, SOX

description: Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis must accompany each shipment to this customer, confirming that the chemical product meets specified quality and composition standards.
reference: ISO 9001, GMP, LabWare LIMS, SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Indicates whether a Certificate of Compliance is required with each shipment, attesting that the product conforms to applicable regulatory and customer-specified requirements.
reference: ISO 9001, SAP S/4HANA QM, ACC Responsible Care

description: Timestamp when this regulatory profile record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail for compliance record-keeping purposes.
reference: ISO 9001, SOX, SAP S/4HANA SD, Salesforce CRM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of destination for chemical product shipments to this customer. Drives applicable regulatory framework selection (e.g., REACH for EU, TSCA for USA).
reference: ISO 3166-1, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, DOT, GHS

description: Geographic region or trade bloc of the customer destination (e.g., European Union, North America, Asia-Pacific) used for regional regulatory framework grouping and reporting.

Indicates whether this customer qualifies as a REACH downstream user and has been notified of their obligations under REACH, including use conditions, exposure scenarios, and risk management measures.
Date from which this regulatory profile becomes binding and governs chemical product sales and documentation obligations for the customer.

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Certification Expiry Date
description: Expiry date of the customer's EHS certification. Triggers re-validation workflow when approaching expiry to ensure continued compliance eligibility.

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Certification Number
description: Certificate number or registration identifier for the customer's EHS management system certification (e.g., ISO 14001 certificate number), used for audit trail and verification.
reference: ISO 14001, ISO 45001, OSHA, Intelex EHS Management

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Certification Status
description: Current status of the customer's EHS certification relevant to handling and using chemical products (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 45001, OSHA VPP). Determines eligibility for certain product categories.
reference: ISO 14001, ISO 45001, OSHA, Intelex EHS Management, ACC Responsible Care

description: Customer's declared intended end-use application for the chemical products purchased (e.g., industrial solvent, agricultural formulation, polymer additive, pharmaceutical intermediate). Required for REACH downstream user obligations and export control compliance.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, ECHA, EAR

description: Industry sector classification of the customer's end-use application for purchased chemicals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|construction|electronics|agriculture|pharmaceuticals|consumer_goods|coatings|textiles|food_contact|industrial — promote to reference product]
Date on which this regulatory profile expires and must be renewed or re-validated. Null indicates an open-ended profile with no scheduled expiry.
Export license or authorization number issued by the originating country's regulatory authority for controlled chemical substances, required for dual-use or restricted chemicals.
reference: EAR (Export Administration Regulations), TSCA, DOT, EPA

description: Indicates whether the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals applies to products sold to this customer, requiring GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheets and labels.
reference: GHS (UN Globally Harmonized System), OSHA HazCom 2012, REACH CLP Regulation

description: DOT/IATA/IMDG hazardous materials transport classification applicable to chemical products shipped to this customer (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 8 Corrosive). Governs packaging, labeling, and shipping documentation requirements.
reference: DOT 49 CFR, IATA DGR, IMDG Code, NFPA, Oracle Transportation Management

description: Customer's import license or permit number issued by the destination country's regulatory authority, required for controlled or restricted chemical substances. Must be validated before shipment.
reference: TSCA, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, DOT, ECHA, EPA

description: Date of the most recent regulatory compliance audit conducted at or for this customer. Used to schedule next audit and assess compliance currency.
reference: ISO 9001, ISO 14001, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, Intelex EHS Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this regulatory profile record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and downstream data synchronization.

business_glossary_term: Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the legacy MSDS format (pre-GHS) is required in addition to or instead of the current SDS format, applicable for customers in jurisdictions that have not fully adopted GHS.
Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this regulatory profile to ensure continued accuracy, completeness, and alignment with current regulatory requirements.
reference: ISO 9001, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA, ACC Responsible Care

description: Externally-known business identifier for this regulatory profile, used in correspondence, audits, and regulatory submissions. Format: REGP-YYYYMMDD-CountryCode.

description: Current lifecycle state of the customer regulatory profile. Active indicates all requirements are met and sales may proceed; pending_review indicates documentation is under assessment; suspended indicates a compliance hold; expired indicates the profile requires renewal.
Classification of the customer relationship type that determines applicable regulatory obligations and documentation requirements for chemical product sales.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, TSCA

description: Indicates whether California Proposition 65 (Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act) warning requirements apply to products sold to this customer based on end-use state or distribution territory.

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether the European Chemicals Agency REACH regulation applies to chemical products sold to this customer, based on destination country and product use.
Email address of the designated regulatory affairs or EHS contact at the customer organization for compliance documentation, SDS distribution, and regulatory notifications.

description: Full name of the designated regulatory affairs or EHS contact person at the customer organization responsible for managing compliance documentation and regulatory correspondence.

description: Comma-separated list of chemical product categories that are restricted or prohibited from sale to this customer based on regulatory, end-use, or jurisdictional constraints. Triggers order block in SAP S/4HANA SD.

ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) specifying the required language for Safety Data Sheets provided to this customer (e.g., en, de, fr, zh-CN).

Primary UN number identifying the hazardous substance or article for transport documentation purposes. Required on shipping papers, labels, and placards for regulated chemical shipments to this customer.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer segment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the customer_segment data product.

Name or employee identifier of the commercial or marketing leader who formally approved the creation or last revision of this customer segment definition. Supports governance and audit trail requirements.

description: Commercial assessment of the revenue growth potential for this customer segment over a 3-5 year horizon: high (significant expansion opportunity), medium (stable moderate growth), low (mature or declining market), emerging (nascent segment with high uncertainty but upside potential). description: Indicates whether products shipped to customers in this segment are classified as hazardous materials requiring DOT/IATA/IMDG compliant shipping documentation, placarding, and carrier certification.
