description: Primary key for service_profile
Reference to the customer account for which this technical service profile is established. Links the service profile to the customer master record in Salesforce CRM.

Reference to the internal employee designated as the primary Technical Service Representative (TSR) for this customer account. The TSR is the main point of contact for day-to-day technical support and application assistance.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Account Team; SAP S/4HANA SD

business_glossary_term: Backup Technical Service Representative (TSR) Employee ID
description: Reference to the secondary or backup Technical Service Representative (TSR) who covers the account in the primary TSR's absence, ensuring continuity of technical support.

Comma-separated list or free-text description of the specific product applications and end-use segments approved for this customer under this service profile (e.g., automotive coatings, agricultural adjuvants, electronic encapsulants). Drives application-specific technical support scope.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; TSCA; ACC Responsible Care

description: Indicates whether Certificates of Analysis (COA) are automatically delivered to the customer with each shipment as part of this service profile. When True, COA documents are generated from LabWare LIMS and transmitted electronically upon shipment confirmation.
reference: ISO 9001 - Clause 8.6; GMP; LabWare LIMS; SAP S/4HANA QM

business_glossary_term: Technical Complaint Response Service Level Agreement (SLA) Days
description: Contracted number of business days within which Chemical Mfg must provide an initial technical response to a customer complaint or out-of-specification (OOS) claim under this service profile. Drives CAPA process timelines.
reference: ISO 9001 - Clause 8.7; CAPA Process; Salesforce CRM - Entitlement SLA

description: Timestamp indicating when this technical service profile record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX - Internal Controls; ISO 9001 - Records Management; GDPR Article 5

description: Business segment classification of the customer receiving technical service. Distinguishes between direct industrial customers, distributors, OEM manufacturers, end-users, and strategic accounts, which determines the nature and depth of technical service provided.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Account Type; ACC Responsible Care

description: Date on which this technical service profile becomes active and binding. Marks the start of the contracted service period.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Contract Start Date; ISO 9001

description: Defined escalation path for technical complaints and out-of-specification (OOS) issues raised by this customer. Specifies the sequence of escalation contacts from TSR through regional management to executive level, aligned with the customer's service tier and criticality.
reference: ISO 9001 - Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs; CAPA Process; Salesforce CRM - Case Escalation

description: Date on which this technical service profile expires or is scheduled to end. Null indicates an open-ended service agreement with no fixed termination date.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Contract End Date; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether the customer is entitled to formulation assistance services under this service profile. When True, Chemical Mfg's formulation scientists provide direct support in developing or optimizing the customer's product formulations using Chemical Mfg materials.
reference: ISO 9001; ACC Responsible Care; Salesforce CRM - Entitlement

description: The primary industry or end-use market segment of the customer (e.g., automotive, construction, electronics, agriculture, consumer goods, coatings, adhesives). Drives application-specific technical support expertise assignment and approved application scope. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|construction|electronics|agriculture|consumer_goods|coatings|adhesives|pharmaceuticals|food_contact|textiles — promote to reference product]
reference: ACC Responsible Care; REACH; TSCA; Salesforce CRM - Industry Field

description: Reference number for the Joint Development Agreement (JDA) between Chemical Mfg and the customer, if applicable. A JDA formalizes collaborative R&D activities, IP ownership, and co-development milestones. Populated only for strategic-tier customers with active joint development programs.
reference: ACC Responsible Care; TSCA; Salesforce CRM - Contract Object

description: Level of laboratory support the customer is entitled to under this service profile. 'None' indicates no lab support; 'Basic' covers standard analytical testing; 'Advanced' includes formulation development support; 'Full' includes dedicated lab time and custom testing via LabWare LIMS.
Timestamp indicating when this technical service profile record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring in the Silver Layer.
reference: SOX - Internal Controls; ISO 9001 - Records Management; GDPR Article 5

description: Date on which this technical service profile was last formally reviewed and validated with the customer, ensuring service entitlements, contacts, and approved applications remain current and accurate.
reference: ISO 9001 - Management Review; Salesforce CRM - Service Contract

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Customer Code
description: Customer identifier in LabWare LIMS used to associate lab test requests, COA generation, and analytical results with this customer's service profile. Enables traceability between lab activities and customer service entitlements.

business_glossary_term: Next Scheduled Service Profile Review Date
description: Date on which the next formal review of this technical service profile is scheduled. Ensures periodic validation of service entitlements and alignment with evolving customer application needs.
reference: ISO 9001 - Management Review; Salesforce CRM - Service Contract

description: Agreed frequency of on-site technical service visits to the customer's facility by the assigned TSR or application engineer. Drives field service scheduling and travel planning.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Contract; ACC Responsible Care Program

description: Indicates whether the customer has been granted access to Chemical Mfg's pilot plant facilities for scale-up trials, process validation, or application development. Typically reserved for strategic-tier customers with active joint development programs.
reference: PSM (Process Safety Management); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119; ACC Responsible Care

description: Business email address of the primary technical contact at the customer organization. Used for technical communications, SDS/COA distribution, and complaint correspondence.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Contact Object; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Full name of the primary technical contact person at the customer's organization who interfaces with Chemical Mfg's technical service team. This is the customer-side counterpart to the assigned TSR.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Contact Object; GDPR Article 4

description: Direct phone number of the primary technical contact at the customer organization for urgent technical support communications.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Contact Object; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this technical service profile, used in customer-facing communications and cross-system references. Sourced from Salesforce CRM service agreement records.
Human-readable name or title for the technical service profile, typically reflecting the customer name and service tier (e.g., 'Acme Corp - Strategic Technical Service Profile').

Free-text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or qualifications relevant to this customer's technical service profile, such as specific handling requirements, regulatory sensitivities, or strategic account notes.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Contract Notes; ISO 9001

description: Current lifecycle status of the technical service profile, indicating whether the service agreement is currently in force, pending activation, suspended, or expired.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Contract Status; ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether this service profile includes regulatory support services, such as assistance with REACH registration dossiers, TSCA compliance documentation, or GHS classification queries. Relevant for customers in regulated end-use markets.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; TSCA; GHS; ECHA

description: Number of days before the profile expiration date that an automated renewal reminder should be triggered to the assigned TSR and account manager. Supports proactive service contract renewal management.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Contract Renewal; ISO 9001

description: Maximum number of product sample requests the customer is entitled to per calendar year under this service profile. Supports the technical sales model where samples are provided for application evaluation and qualification.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Entitlement; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: SAP S/4HANA SD sold-to party number for the customer associated with this service profile. Used to link technical service entitlements to order management, pricing, and delivery processes in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master; SAP BP (Business Partner)

description: Indicates whether Chemical Mfg is responsible for proactively distributing Safety Data Sheets (SDS) to this customer as part of the service profile. When True, updated SDS documents are automatically distributed upon revision.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom Standard; REACH Article 31

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the customer site location where technical services are delivered. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (e.g., REACH for EU, TSCA for USA) and regional TSR assignment.
reference: ISO 3166-1; REACH; TSCA; DOT Hazardous Materials Regulations

description: Name or identifier of the specific customer plant, facility, or site location to which this technical service profile applies. A customer may have multiple sites with different service profiles.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Account Site; SAP S/4HANA SD - Ship-To Party

description: Classification of the level of technical service and application support contracted or agreed upon with the customer. 'Standard' provides basic technical support; 'Premium' includes dedicated resources and faster response; 'Strategic' includes joint development, pilot plant access, and executive engagement. Drives resource allocation and SLA commitments.
reference: ACC Responsible Care Program; Salesforce CRM - Service Level

description: Number of technical training sessions (webinars, workshops, or on-site training) the customer is entitled to per year under this service profile. Covers product application training, safety handling, and regulatory updates.
reference: OSHA Training Requirements; ACC Responsible Care; Salesforce CRM - Entitlement

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the customer service case or support ticket. Primary key for the case data product.
Reference to the customer account that initiated or is associated with this case.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Reference to the chemical product involved in this case, particularly relevant for quality complaints, technical inquiries, and documentation requests (SDS, COA, COC).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; SAP QM

description: Reference to the specific customer contact person who submitted or is the primary point of contact for this case.
Reference to the internal customer service representative or technical service engineer assigned to resolve this case.
reference: Salesforce CRM User Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Invoice Dispute Case Management requires linking each case to the specific invoice it concerns, enabling traceability for financial reconciliation and regulatory reporting.

Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order Issue Management report links each service case to the originating sales order for root‑cause analysis and compliance tracking.
Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Allows linking support cases to related R&D projects, essential for issue escalation and regulatory audit trails.

The internal manufacturing batch reference number associated with the product involved in the case. Distinct from lot_number (which is the customer-facing identifier); batch_reference links to the production batch record in SAP PP and MES systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Batch; MES; GMP

description: Reference number of the CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) record created in SAP QM in response to a quality complaint case. Links the customer case to the formal quality improvement workflow. Populated only when a CAPA has been initiated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM CAPA; ISO 9001 Clause 10.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: Full narrative description of the customer issue, request, or complaint as reported by the customer. For quality complaints, includes the customer's account of the product defect, observed symptoms, and business impact.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Description; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

description: Externally visible, human-readable case reference number used in all customer communications and cross-system references. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Case Number field.

Current workflow state of the case lifecycle. Open: newly created, awaiting assignment. In Progress: actively being worked. Pending Customer: awaiting customer response or information. Resolved: solution provided, awaiting confirmation. Closed: fully completed and confirmed.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Status; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

description: Classification of the customer case by business function. Technical Support covers formulation and application assistance; Order Issue covers shipment discrepancies and billing disputes; SDS (Safety Data Sheet) Request and COA (Certificate of Analysis) Request cover documentation fulfillment; Regulatory Inquiry covers REACH, TSCA, GHS compliance questions; Quality Complaint covers OOS (Out of Specification) product issues.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Type; GHS; REACH; TSCA; ISO 9001

description: The communication channel through which the customer submitted or initiated the case. Supports channel analytics and resource allocation for customer service operations.
The date on which the case was fully closed after customer confirmation of resolution. Distinct from resolved_date; a case may be resolved but remain open pending customer acknowledgment.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Closed Date; ISO 9001

description: Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document associated with the product lot involved in the case. Used for quality complaints to cross-reference the analytical results at time of shipment against the customer's reported issue.
reference: LabWare LIMS COA; ISO 9001 Clause 8.6; GMP

description: The date and time when the case record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and is used for SLA calculation baseline.

Indicates whether a financial credit note has been issued to the customer as part of the resolution of a quality complaint or order discrepancy case. When True, a corresponding credit memo exists in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Credit Memo; SAP SD; SOX

description: The SAP document number of the credit note issued to the customer for a quality complaint or order discrepancy resolution. Populated only when credit_note_issued is True. Enables financial reconciliation between the case and the accounting entry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; SAP SD Credit Memo; SOX; FASB

description: The native case identifier from Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer cases. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system.

Categorical outcome of the customer satisfaction assessment following case closure. Derived from the customer's qualitative feedback or survey response. Complements the numeric customer_satisfaction_rating for segmentation and reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM; ISO 9001 Clause 9.1.2; ACC Responsible Care

description: Numeric customer satisfaction score collected after case resolution, typically on a 1-5 scale (1=Very Dissatisfied, 5=Very Satisfied). Sourced from post-resolution customer survey. Used as a KPI (Key Performance Indicator) for customer service quality.

Reference number of the 8D (Eight Disciplines) problem-solving report generated for a quality complaint case. The 8D methodology is the standard structured problem-solving workflow for quality complaints in chemical manufacturing. Populated when an 8D report has been formally initiated.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 10.2; SAP QM; AIAG 8D Standard; ACC Responsible Care

description: Indicates whether the case has been formally escalated beyond the initially assigned service representative, either due to SLA breach, severity, or customer request. Escalated cases trigger management notification workflows.
The date on which the case was formally escalated. Populated only when escalated is True. Used to measure escalation response time and identify systemic SLA failure patterns.

The reason the case was escalated. SLA Breach: resolution deadline exceeded. Customer Request: customer demanded escalation. Safety Concern: potential EHS (Environment Health and Safety) implication. Regulatory Risk: potential regulatory non-compliance. Management Directive: escalated by management decision.

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Investigation ID
description: The investigation record identifier in LabWare LIMS linked to this case for OOS (Out of Specification) product investigations. Populated when an OOS investigation has been initiated in the LIMS system in response to a quality complaint.
reference: LabWare LIMS OOS Investigation; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; GMP

description: The manufacturing lot or batch lot number of the chemical product involved in a quality complaint or technical inquiry. Enables traceability back to the production batch for root cause investigation. Populated primarily for quality_complaint and technical_support case types.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Batch Management; LabWare LIMS; GMP; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.2

description: The date and time when the case record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse.

Indicates whether the quality complaint involves a product that has been confirmed as Out of Specification (OOS) against the product's Certificate of Analysis (COA) or specification sheet. When True, triggers mandatory OOS investigation workflow in LabWare LIMS.
reference: LabWare LIMS OOS Investigation; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; GMP; FDA

description: The calendar date on which the customer case was officially opened and entered into the system. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) calculation and aging analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Created Date; ISO 9001

description: Business priority level assigned to the case, driving SLA (Service Level Agreement) response and resolution targets. Critical priority is reserved for safety-related quality complaints or regulatory non-compliance issues.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Priority; OSHA PSM; ISO 9001

description: Current status of the Root Cause Investigation (RCI) triggered by a quality complaint. Tracks whether the investigation into the product defect or OOS (Out of Specification) condition has been initiated, is ongoing, or has been completed. Applicable only to quality_complaint case type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; LabWare LIMS; ISO 9001 Clause 10.2; CAPA

description: The specific regulatory framework or standard that is the subject of a regulatory inquiry case. Identifies whether the customer's inquiry relates to REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals), TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act), GHS (Globally Harmonized System), or other applicable regulation. Populated for regulatory_inquiry case type.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; TSCA; GHS; ECHA; EPA

description: Detailed narrative of the actions taken and solution provided to resolve the customer case. For quality complaints, includes findings from the RCI (Root Cause Investigation) and corrective actions applied.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Resolution; ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; CAPA

description: The date on which the case was marked as resolved, meaning the solution or response was provided to the customer. Used to calculate actual resolution time against SLA targets.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Closed Date; ISO 9001

description: The document control number of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) provided or referenced in response to an SDS request case. Enables traceability of which SDS version was fulfilled. Applicable to sds_request case type.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System) SDS Requirements; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; REACH Article 31; ECHA

description: Severity classification of the issue impact on the customer's operations or safety. Distinct from priority (which drives internal response urgency), severity reflects the actual business or safety impact of the reported issue. Critical severity triggers mandatory CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) and RCI (Root Cause Investigation) workflows.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.7; SAP QM Defect Classification; OSHA

description: Indicates whether the case resolution exceeded the SLA due date, constituting an SLA breach. Used for service quality reporting, customer penalty tracking, and continuous improvement initiatives.
The contractual or policy-defined deadline by which the case must be resolved, based on case type, priority, and applicable customer SLA agreement. Drives escalation workflows when breached.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case SLA; ISO 9001 Clause 8.2

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this case record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA; Salesforce CRM; LabWare LIMS

description: Brief, descriptive title summarizing the customer issue or request. Used as the primary display label in case management queues and customer communications.
Unique surrogate identifier for the contact person record in the customer domain silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the parent customer account this contact is associated with. Links the contact to the B2B industrial customer, distributor, or end-user account.

Reference to the specific customer site or plant location this contact is associated with. A customer account may have multiple plant sites; this links the contact to their operational location for logistics and technical service routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; Salesforce CRM Account Site

description: Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Needed for Sponsor Contact Management process to associate external customer contacts with the R&D projects they fund, supporting contract compliance and communication.

Preferred delivery method for Certificate of Analysis (COA) and Certificate of Compliance (COC) documents for this contact. Drives automated document distribution workflows in the quality management process.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; SAP S/4HANA QM; LabWare LIMS

description: Functional role classification of the contact in relation to Chemical Mfg business interactions. Drives workflow routing for technical sales, EHS (Environment Health and Safety) compliance, logistics coordination, and quality inquiries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: technical|commercial|ehs|logistics|quality|procurement|finance|executive — promote to reference product if additional roles are needed]
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; ACC Responsible Care Program

description: Current lifecycle status of the contact record. 'active' indicates the contact is reachable and engaged; 'inactive' indicates the contact is no longer in the role; 'do_not_contact' enforces communication suppression; 'pending_verification' indicates the record awaits validation.
reference: GDPR Article 17; CCPA Section 1798.105; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Classification of the contact by their relationship to Chemical Mfg. 'internal' denotes Chemical Mfg employees; 'external' denotes customer personnel; 'distributor' denotes channel partner contacts; 'end_user' denotes final consumer contacts; 'consultant' denotes third-party advisors.
Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the silver layer data product. Supports audit trail requirements, GDPR data retention tracking, and data lineage for the customer domain.
Unique identifier for this contact record in Salesforce CRM, the system of record for customer contact management. Used for bi-directional synchronization between the lakehouse and Salesforce.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the contact's purchase order authority limit (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures correct monetary interpretation across multi-currency customer accounts.

Organizational department or functional area the contact belongs to within the customer organization (e.g., Procurement, Engineering, Quality Assurance, Environment Health and Safety, Logistics, R&D). Supports targeted communication routing.

Indicates whether this contact has opted out of or been suppressed from all outbound communications. When True, no marketing, sales, or non-essential operational communications should be sent. Enforces GDPR and CCPA consent management requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 7 and Article 17; CCPA Section 1798.120; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Reason code explaining why the do_not_contact_flag is set to True. Supports regulatory audit trails for GDPR right-to-erasure and CCPA opt-out compliance. Null when do_not_contact_flag is False.

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Contact Flag
description: Indicates whether this contact is the designated EHS (Environment Health and Safety) coordinator or responsible person at the customer site. EHS contacts receive hazardous material notifications, incident reports, and regulatory compliance updates.
reference: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration); EPA (Environmental Protection Agency); Intelex EHS Management; ISO 45001

description: Primary business email address of the contact. Used for order confirmations, technical documentation delivery, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) distribution, Certificate of Analysis (COA) transmission, and regulatory correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Alternate or secondary email address for the contact. Used as a fallback communication channel when the primary email is undeliverable, or for routing specific document types (e.g., invoices to a separate accounts payable email).
Given name of the contact person. Used in personalized communications, technical sales outreach, and customer service workflows.

Indicates whether this contact holds purchasing or approval authority within their organization. Used by technical sales teams to prioritize engagement and qualify opportunities in the chemical sales cycle.

Indicates whether this contact is the designated primary contact for the associated customer account. Only one contact per customer account should be flagged as primary. Used to determine the default recipient for account-level communications.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

description: Official job title or position of the contact within their organization (e.g., Procurement Manager, Plant Engineer, Quality Manager, EHS Coordinator, Technical Buyer). Used for routing communications and identifying decision-makers.

Preferred language for communications with this contact, expressed as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'fr', 'zh-CN'). Ensures SDS, COA, and regulatory documents are delivered in the correct language per REACH and GHS requirements.
reference: IETF BCP 47; GHS (Globally Harmonized System); REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Date of the most recent recorded business interaction with this contact (e.g., sales call, technical inquiry, order placement, site visit). Used to measure contact engagement recency and identify dormant contacts for re-engagement campaigns.
Family name or surname of the contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for business correspondence and CRM records.

LinkedIn public profile URL for the contact. Used by technical sales and account management teams for relationship intelligence, pre-call research, and identifying decision-maker networks within B2B industrial customer organizations.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; GDPR Article 4

description: Middle name or initial of the contact person. Used for disambiguation when multiple contacts share the same first and last name within a customer organization.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Direct office or desk phone number for the contact in E.164 international format. Used for urgent technical support, order management, and regulatory compliance communications.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; ITU-T E.164

description: Fax number for the contact in E.164 international format. Relevant for regulatory document transmission (e.g., SDS, COA, purchase orders) in industries and regions where fax remains a compliance-accepted channel.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ITU-T E.164; REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Mobile or cell phone number for the contact in E.164 international format. Used for SMS notifications, emergency EHS (Environment Health and Safety) alerts, and field-level technical support.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; ITU-T E.164

description: The contact's preferred channel for receiving business communications from Chemical Mfg. Drives outbound communication routing in Salesforce CRM and customer service workflows.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; GDPR Article 7

description: Maximum monetary value in the customer's base currency that this contact is authorized to approve on a Purchase Order (PO). Used by the technical sales team to identify the appropriate contact for order approvals and to escalate large orders to higher-authority contacts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FASB

description: Indicates whether this contact should receive regulatory compliance notifications, including TSCA updates, REACH substance restriction alerts, EPA reporting deadlines, and product reformulation notices. Supports proactive regulatory communication workflows.
reference: TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act); REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; EPA Regulations; ECHA

description: Date on which this contact's association with the customer account was terminated (e.g., due to role change, departure, or account closure). Null for currently active contacts. Used for historical record retention and compliance audit trails.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (storage limitation); Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Date on which this contact was first established as an active contact for the customer account. Used to calculate relationship tenure and for historical analysis of contact network evolution.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Formal salutation or honorific prefix for the contact (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr., Prof.). Used in formal business correspondence, regulatory submissions, and technical documentation.

Indicates whether this contact has consented to receive Safety Data Sheet (SDS) and Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) documents electronically. Required for OSHA HazCom 2012 and GHS compliance in chemical product distribution.
reference: OSHA HazCom 2012 (29 CFR 1910.1200); GHS (Globally Harmonized System); REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006

description: Identifies the originating operational system from which this contact record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation between Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA SD.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; ISO/IEC 11179

description: Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive technical service support, including application engineering assistance, formulation guidance, process troubleshooting, and Certificate of Analysis (COA) requests.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this contact record in the silver layer. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.
Unique surrogate identifier for the customer lead record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the customer_lead data product. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead object.

Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated or influenced this lead. Used for campaign ROI measurement, marketing attribution, and budget allocation decisions. References the Salesforce Campaign object.

Salesforce Account ID of the customer account created upon lead conversion. Populated only when lead_status = 'converted'. Enables traceability from lead origin to active customer account for pipeline attribution and cohort analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Salesforce Opportunity ID created upon lead conversion. Populated only when lead_status = 'converted'. Links the lead origin to the downstream commercial deal for end-to-end pipeline attribution.
reference: Salesforce CRM Opportunity Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Salesforce User ID of the sales representative currently responsible for working this lead. Used for workload balancing, performance reporting, and commission attribution.
reference: Salesforce CRM User Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Estimated annual revenue band of the prospective customer's organization (e.g., '<$10M', '$10M–$50M', '$50M–$250M', '>$250M'). Used for strategic account tiering and prioritization. Sourced from third-party firmographic data or sales rep estimation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: <$10M|$10M-$50M|$50M-$250M|$250M-$1B|>$1B — promote to reference product]
City of the prospective customer's primary operating location. Used for geographic clustering analysis and logistics proximity assessment for potential supply chain planning.

Legal or trading name of the prospective customer organization. Represents the business entity being targeted for chemical product sales. Used for account matching and deduplication prior to conversion.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; ACC Responsible Care Program

description: Categorical classification of the prospective customer's organization size. 'small' = <50 employees, 'medium' = 50–499, 'large' = 500–4999, 'enterprise' = 5000+. Used for segmentation, pricing strategy, and sales motion selection (transactional vs. strategic).
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; ACC Responsible Care Program

description: First name of the primary contact person at the prospective customer organization. Captured from trade show registrations, web inquiries, referrals, or cold outreach.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM Lead Object

description: Last name of the primary contact person at the prospective customer organization. Combined with contact_first_name to form the full contact identity.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM Lead Object

description: Professional job title or role of the primary contact at the prospective customer (e.g., Procurement Manager, R&D Director, Plant Engineer). Informs sales approach and technical engagement strategy.

Date on which the lead was converted into a formal customer account and/or sales opportunity in the CRM. Marks the end of the lead lifecycle and the beginning of the account/opportunity lifecycle. Used to calculate lead-to-conversion cycle time KPI.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the country of the prospective customer's primary operating location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Used for geographic segmentation, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and export compliance screening.

Timestamp when the customer lead record was first created in the source Salesforce CRM system. Represents the moment the lead entered the pipeline. Used for lead age calculation, SLA tracking, and pipeline velocity reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Native Salesforce CRM Lead record identifier (18-character Salesforce ID). Used for traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.

Reason code or description explaining why the lead was disqualified from the pipeline (e.g., 'no budget', 'competitor locked in', 'wrong product fit', 'regulatory barrier', 'no decision maker access'). Populated only when lead_status = 'disqualified'. Used for pipeline quality analysis and process improvement.
Primary business email address of the lead contact. Used for digital outreach, campaign tracking, and CRM communication logging. Subject to GDPR and CCPA consent requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM Lead Object

description: Estimated annual revenue potential from this lead expressed in US dollars. Derived from volume estimates and indicative pricing. Used for pipeline valuation, sales target tracking, and territory planning. Distinct from volume as it reflects commercial value.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; FASB; SOX

description: Prospective annual purchase volume of chemical products expressed in kilograms. Used to prioritize lead qualification effort, assess strategic value, and forecast pipeline contribution. Captured during initial qualification conversation or estimated from industry benchmarks.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Indicates whether the prospective customer or the chemical products of interest are subject to export control regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR, or dual-use chemical restrictions). When True, requires screening against denied party lists before conversion and shipment.
reference: DOT; EPA; TSCA; EAR (Export Administration Regulations); ITAR

description: The end-market industry sector of the prospective customer (e.g., Automotive, Construction, Electronics, Consumer Goods, Agriculture, Pharmaceuticals, Coatings). Used for market segmentation, product-to-market alignment, and territory planning. Aligns with NAICS/SIC classification.
reference: NAICS Industry Classification; Salesforce CRM Lead Object

description: The originating channel or method through which the lead was acquired. Used for marketing attribution, ROI analysis, and sales pipeline source reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trade_show|referral|web_inquiry|cold_outreach|distributor|conference|digital_campaign|inbound_call — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; ACC Responsible Care Program

description: Current stage of the lead in the sales qualification lifecycle. 'new' = recently captured, 'working' = active outreach in progress, 'qualified' = meets criteria for conversion, 'converted' = promoted to formal account/opportunity, 'disqualified' = does not meet criteria, 'recycled' = re-entered pipeline after prior disqualification. Drives CRM workflow automation.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; ACC Responsible Care Program

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the customer lead record in the source Salesforce CRM system. Used for incremental data ingestion, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Scheduled date for the next sales outreach or follow-up action with this lead. Used by sales representatives to manage their activity pipeline and ensure timely engagement. Drives CRM task and reminder generation.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Free-text field capturing qualitative context about the lead, including conversation summaries, specific application requirements, competitive intelligence, or special handling instructions. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead Description field.
Primary business phone number of the lead contact. Used for direct sales outreach and qualification calls.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Salesforce CRM Lead Object

description: The chemical product line, product family, or specific chemical compound the lead has expressed interest in (e.g., specialty polymers, performance coatings, agricultural intermediates, solvents). Drives assignment to the appropriate technical sales specialist.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Date on which the lead was formally assessed and determined to meet the minimum criteria for advancement in the sales pipeline (e.g., confirmed budget, authority, need, and timeline — BANT qualification). Marks transition from 'new' or 'working' to 'qualified' status.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Sales representative's qualitative assessment of the lead's near-term conversion likelihood. 'hot' = high urgency and fit, 'warm' = moderate interest, 'cold' = low engagement or long-horizon. Used to prioritize outreach cadence.
Name of the individual, company, or partner who referred this lead. Populated when lead_source = 'referral'. Used for referral program tracking and partner relationship management.

Indicates whether the lead involves products or applications subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny (e.g., REACH-restricted substances, TSCA new chemical notifications, EPA hazardous waste regulations, export-controlled chemicals). When True, triggers compliance review before conversion.
Code identifying the geographic or market-based sales territory to which this lead is assigned. Used for territory performance reporting, quota allocation, and rep assignment routing.
reference: Salesforce CRM Territory Management; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Indicates whether the lead contact has requested a product sample for laboratory evaluation or formulation testing. Sample requests indicate active technical qualification and are a key pipeline progression signal in chemical B2B sales.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SAP S/4HANA SD; LabWare LIMS

description: Numeric score assigned by the CRM marketing automation engine reflecting the lead's engagement level and fit with ideal customer profile. Typically ranges 0–100. Inputs include email opens, web visits, content downloads, and firmographic match. Used to trigger automated nurture workflows.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; Salesforce Marketing Cloud

description: Indicates whether the lead contact has requested a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for a chemical product of interest. SDS requests are a strong buying signal in chemical sales and indicate technical evaluation stage. Aligns with GHS SDS documentation requirements.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; Salesforce CRM Lead Object

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this lead record was sourced. For the customer_lead product, this is always 'salesforce_crm'. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: State or province of the prospective customer's primary operating location. Used for regional sales analysis, territory assignment, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping (e.g., state-level EPA or OSHA requirements).
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; EPA; OSHA

description: Indicates whether the lead has submitted a technical inquiry requiring specialist response (e.g., formulation compatibility, regulatory compliance documentation, application engineering support). Triggers routing to technical sales or application development team.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Name of the trade show, industry conference, or event at which the lead was captured (e.g., 'ChemShow 2024', 'ACHEMA', 'ACS National Meeting'). Populated when lead_source = 'trade_show' or 'conference'. Used for event ROI analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM Lead Object; ACC Responsible Care Program

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each customer interaction record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the interaction data product.
Reference to the customer (account) involved in this interaction. Links to the customer master data product.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

description: Reference to the customer service case or complaint record linked to this interaction, if applicable. Supports complaint resolution tracking and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) workflows.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Object; Intelex EHS CAPA Module

description: Foreign key linking to product.chemical_product. Business justification: Supports support interaction logs tying each customer interaction to the specific chemical product discussed, required for service quality metrics.
Reference to the specific customer contact person who participated in this interaction. Links to the customer contact data product.
Reference to the specific customer site or plant location where the interaction occurred or is relevant to. Supports site-level account management and technical service tracking.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Site; SAP S/4HANA SD Ship-To Party

description: Reference to the internal employee (account manager or sales representative) who owns the customer account and is responsible for this interaction.
reference: Salesforce CRM User Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Representative

description: Reference to the sales opportunity linked to this interaction, if applicable. Enables tracking of interactions that advance a specific sales pursuit.

Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Interaction Log per Order ties customer/technical interactions to a specific sales order, enabling SLA measurement and order‑level support metrics.

Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Enables Interaction‑Project Mapping report, tying customer interactions (meetings, calls) to specific R&D projects for account planning and ROI analysis.
The communication channel through which the interaction was conducted. Supports omnichannel analytics and customer engagement reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Channel; ISO 9001 Customer Communication

description: Indicates whether the customer requested a Certificate of Analysis (COA) during this interaction. Supports quality document fulfillment tracking and customer quality management.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management; LabWare LIMS COA Module

description: Indicates whether this interaction involves or originated from a customer complaint. Triggers linkage to the complaint/case management workflow and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) process.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2 Customer Satisfaction; Salesforce CRM Case

description: The date and time when this interaction record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: Salesforce CRM CreatedDate; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: External identifier from Salesforce CRM activity record. Used to trace the interaction back to the system of record for reconciliation and lineage.
Number of customer-side contacts who participated in the interaction. Indicates the breadth of customer engagement and stakeholder involvement.
Comma-separated list of customer contact names or identifiers who participated in the interaction. Confidential business relationship data.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Invitees; GDPR Article 4

description: Numeric satisfaction rating provided by the customer or assessed by the account manager following the interaction, typically on a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Supports Key Performance Indicator (KPI) tracking for customer relationship health.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Clause 9.1.2 Customer Satisfaction; ACC Responsible Care

description: The total elapsed time of the interaction in minutes. Used to assess engagement depth, resource utilization, and account planning efficiency.
The target date by which the required follow-up action must be completed. Used for task management and account manager accountability tracking.
Free-text description of the specific follow-up actions committed to during the interaction. Captures commitments made to the customer for accountability and continuity.
Indicates whether a follow-up action or subsequent interaction is required as a result of this interaction. Drives task creation and account planning workflows.
The calendar date on which the customer interaction occurred. Used for day-level scheduling, account planning, and reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Date; ISO 9001 Customer Communication Records

description: Detailed narrative of the interaction content, discussion points, and context. Captures technical topics discussed, products referenced, and any regulatory or compliance matters raised.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Description; ISO 9001 Customer Communication Records

description: Current lifecycle status of the interaction record. Indicates whether the interaction was completed as planned, cancelled, or requires rescheduling.
The precise date and time at which the customer interaction commenced. Supports time-zone-aware analytics and SLA measurement.

Classification of the nature or purpose of the customer interaction. Distinguishes between sales-driven, technical service, complaint handling, and digital engagement touchpoints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_call|technical_visit|complaint_call|email_exchange|webinar|trade_show|portal_login|video_conference — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Type; ACC Responsible Care Customer Engagement Guidelines

description: Number of internal Chemical Mfg employees who participated in the interaction. Used for resource utilization and engagement intensity analysis.

Comma-separated list of internal employee names or identifiers who participated in the interaction (e.g., account manager, technical service representative, application chemist). Supports multi-participant interaction tracking.

Physical or virtual location where the interaction took place (e.g., customer plant site, trade show venue, video conference URL, Chemical Mfg office). Relevant for in-person and on-site technical visits.
The date and time when this interaction record was last updated in the system. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.
reference: Salesforce CRM LastModifiedDate; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: The scheduled date for the next planned customer interaction as agreed during this touchpoint. Supports account planning cadence and proactive customer engagement management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity; ACC Responsible Care Account Planning

description: The result or disposition of the interaction as assessed by the internal participant. Supports win/loss analysis, customer satisfaction tracking, and account health scoring.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Result; ACC Responsible Care Customer Satisfaction

description: Business priority level assigned to the interaction, reflecting urgency of follow-up or strategic importance of the customer engagement.

The Chemical Mfg product line or portfolio segment discussed during the interaction (e.g., performance materials, agricultural solutions, specialty chemicals). Supports product-level engagement analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Product Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM Product

description: Indicates whether the interaction involved discussion of regulatory or compliance topics such as REACH, TSCA, GHS, SDS requirements, or EPA/OSHA obligations. Supports regulatory engagement tracking and compliance reporting.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; TSCA; EPA; OSHA; GHS

description: The sales territory code associated with this interaction, derived from the account owner's territory assignment. Supports territory-level performance reporting and account planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Sales Territory; Salesforce CRM Territory Management

description: Indicates whether the customer requested a product sample during this interaction. Triggers linkage to the sample request fulfillment process in the sales domain.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity; SAP S/4HANA SD Sample Management

description: Indicates whether the customer requested a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) during this interaction. Supports regulatory document fulfillment tracking per GHS and OSHA HazCom requirements.
reference: OSHA HazCom Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS; REACH Article 31

description: The originating operational system from which this interaction record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration governance.
reference: Salesforce CRM; SAP S/4HANA SD; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: Brief title or subject line summarizing the topic of the interaction. Mirrors the Salesforce CRM activity subject field and supports keyword search and categorization.

The primary technical subject matter discussed during the interaction (e.g., formulation support, application development, regulatory compliance, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) review, Certificate of Analysis (COA) query). Supports technical service analytics.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity; ACC Responsible Care Technical Service

description: Primary key for status_history
Reference to the customer account whose status changed. Links this history record to the master customer account entity.
Reference to the internal user or system account that initiated the status change. Used for audit trail and accountability tracking in credit department compliance reviews.
reference: SOX Section 302 - Internal Controls; ISO 27001 - Access Control

description: Reference to the original account_status_history_id that this record reverses or corrects. Null when is_reversal is False. Enables complete audit chain reconstruction for compliance inquiries.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Document Reversal

description: Reference to the approval workflow record or authorization document that sanctioned this status change. Required for credit department audit compliance and segregation of duties verification.

External or human-readable reference number of the approval document, workflow ticket, or authorization memo that sanctioned this status change. Complements the approval_reference_id for audit trail documentation and cross-system traceability.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Workflow Reference

description: The SAP billing block code applied to or removed from the customer account as part of this status change. Null when no billing block change occurred. Supports accounts receivable and revenue recognition impact analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Billing Block (FAKSD); FASB ASC 606 - Revenue Recognition

description: The number of open sales orders that were blocked or released as a direct result of this status change. Captured as a snapshot at transition time to quantify the operational impact of credit hold and suspension events.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Order Block (AUFSD); SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Management

business_glossary_term: Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) Reference Number
description: Reference to a CAPA record when the status change was triggered by a quality or compliance corrective action outcome. Links customer account standing changes to the quality management system for traceability.
reference: ISO 9001:2015 Section 10.2 - Corrective Action; SAP S/4HANA QM; Intelex EHS Management

description: Standardized code identifying the business reason for the status transition (e.g., CREDIT_REVIEW, INSOLVENCY, REACTIVATION, REGULATORY_ACTION, MA_ACTIVITY, PAYMENT_DEFAULT, CUSTOMER_REQUEST). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CREDIT_REVIEW|INSOLVENCY|REACTIVATION|REGULATORY_ACTION|MA_ACTIVITY|PAYMENT_DEFAULT|CUSTOMER_REQUEST|CAPA_OUTCOME — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Management; FASB ASC 310

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context for the status change beyond the standardized reason code. Captures nuanced circumstances such as specific regulatory action details, M&A transaction references, or credit review findings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Change Reason Text; SOX Section 302

description: The precise date and time when the status change was recorded in the system. Serves as the EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this event log record. Distinct from effective_date which represents the business effective date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA - Change Document Timestamp; ISO 8601; SOX Section 302

description: Additional free-text commentary entered by the initiating user or approver providing supplementary context for the status change. May include credit analyst notes, legal counsel observations, or customer service remarks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Management Notes; Salesforce CRM - Activity Comments

description: The SAP company code associated with this status change record. Identifies the legal entity within the chemical manufacturing enterprise for which the customer account status was changed. Supports multi-entity financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Company Code (BUKRS); FASB ASC 810; SOX

description: The timestamp when this status history record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail completeness, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.

business_glossary_term: Credit Exposure at Time of Change
description: The customer's total outstanding credit exposure (open orders + open invoices + deliveries) in the base currency at the time of this status change. Provides context for credit hold and suspension decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Exposure (FD33); FASB ASC 310

business_glossary_term: Credit Limit at Time of Change
description: The customer's approved credit limit in the base currency at the time of this status change. Captured as a snapshot to support credit department audit compliance and historical credit exposure analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Management (FD32); FASB ASC 310 - Receivables

description: The credit risk classification assigned to the customer at the time of this status change. Captured as a snapshot to support credit portfolio risk analysis and audit trail for credit hold decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Risk Category (FD32); FASB ASC 310; ACC Responsible Care

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit limit and credit exposure amounts captured in this status history record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes; FASB ASC 830 - Foreign Currency

description: The number of calendar days the customer account remained in the previous status before this transition. Captured at the time of transition for customer lifecycle analytics and credit risk reporting. Not a calculated metric — stored as a business fact at transition time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Management Analytics; FASB ASC 310

description: The SAP delivery block code applied to or removed from the customer account as part of this status change (e.g., 01 for credit block). Null when no delivery block change occurred. Supports supply chain impact analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Block (LIFSP); SAP EWM

description: The business date on which the new status became operationally effective. Used for customer lifecycle analytics, credit reporting, and regulatory inquiry responses. Distinct from the record creation timestamp.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Validity Date; FASB ASC 310 - Receivables

description: The business date on which this status record was superseded by a subsequent status change. Null if this is the current active status record. Enables point-in-time status reconstruction for audit and analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Validity End Date; ISO 8601

description: Court case number, bankruptcy filing reference, or insolvency proceeding identifier when the status change was triggered by an insolvency filing. Null for non-insolvency transitions. Critical for accounts receivable risk management.
reference: FASB ASC 310 - Receivables; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Management

description: Indicates whether this status change record represents a reversal or correction of a previously recorded status transition. True when the record corrects an erroneous prior status change. Used for audit trail integrity and credit department compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302 - Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Reversal Processing

description: Reference identifier for the merger, acquisition, divestiture, or corporate restructuring event that triggered this status change. Null for non-M&A transitions. Supports customer hierarchy realignment and account consolidation tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM - Account Merge

description: The customer account status assigned as a result of this transition event. Captures the 'to' state in the status lifecycle transition pair (e.g., credit_hold in a transition from active→credit_hold).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Account Status; Salesforce CRM - Account Status

description: The date on which the customer was formally notified of the status change. Null when notification_sent is False. Used to track compliance with contractual notification obligations and credit department procedures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Correspondence Date; Salesforce CRM

description: Indicates whether a formal notification was sent to the customer regarding this status change. Relevant for credit hold and suspension events where customer communication is required per contractual or regulatory obligations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Communication; Salesforce CRM - Activity Log

description: The SAP payment terms code (e.g., NT30, NT60, ZB14) applicable to the customer at the time of this status change. Captures any payment terms adjustment associated with the status transition such as tightening terms on credit hold.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Payment Terms (ZTERM); FASB ASC 310

description: The customer account status immediately before this transition event. Captures the 'from' state in the status lifecycle transition pair (e.g., active in a transition from active→credit_hold).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Account Status; Salesforce CRM - Account Status

description: Indicates whether this status change was triggered by or is associated with a REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals) compliance issue with the customer. Supports ECHA regulatory reporting and EHS compliance tracking.
reference: ECHA REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; Intelex EHS Management

description: The governing body or regulatory authority whose action triggered this status change. Applicable when triggering_business_event is 'regulatory_action'. Supports regulatory inquiry responses and EHS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPA|OSHA|ECHA|FDA|DOT|TSCA|ACC|NFPA|OTHER — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: EPA - Environmental Protection Agency; OSHA 29 CFR; ECHA REACH Regulation; TSCA; FDA 21 CFR

description: External regulatory case number, enforcement action identifier, or compliance filing reference associated with this status change when triggered by a regulatory action (e.g., EPA enforcement, TSCA violation, OSHA citation). Null for non-regulatory transitions.
reference: EPA - Enforcement and Compliance; TSCA Section 5; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: The scheduled date by which this status should be reviewed for potential change (e.g., credit hold review date, suspension expiry review). Supports proactive credit management and customer lifecycle workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Review Schedule; FASB ASC 310

description: The SAP sales organization code under which this status change was processed. Relevant for multi-org chemical manufacturing enterprises where customer account status may vary by sales organization.

The operational system of record that originated this status change event. Identifies whether the change was initiated in SAP S/4HANA credit management, Salesforce CRM, manual entry, or another integrated system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce CRM; Intelex EHS Management

description: The native change document number or record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., SAP change document number, Salesforce record ID). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA - Change Document (CDHDR/CDPOS); Salesforce CRM - Audit Trail

description: Categorical classification of the direction and nature of the status change. Distinguishes upgrades (e.g., prospect→active, credit_hold→active) from downgrades (e.g., active→credit_hold, active→suspended) and closures for lifecycle analytics.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Account Lifecycle; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Classification of the specific business event that triggered this status transition. Distinguishes between credit review outcomes, M&A activity, insolvency filings, regulatory actions, and other lifecycle events for customer analytics and compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_review|ma_activity|insolvency_filing|regulatory_action|payment_default|customer_request|periodic_review|reactivation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - Credit Management; Salesforce CRM - Account Events; FASB ASC 310

description: The timestamp when this status history record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks corrections, reversals, or enrichment updates applied after initial ingestion.
Unique surrogate identifier for the customer site or plant location record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the site entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master (Ship-To Party / Delivery Address)

description: Reference to the parent corporate account (customer master) that owns this site. One account may have many sites. Supports customer hierarchy navigation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM - Account Object

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Site‑level operating cost allocation reports need each site tied to a cost center for accurate expense tracking.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Site Manager assignment needed for daily operations, safety compliance, and site performance reporting.
Self-referencing identifier for the parent site in a site hierarchy (e.g., a campus or complex with multiple sub-sites). Enables multi-level site hierarchy modeling for large industrial customers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Hierarchy; Salesforce CRM - Account Hierarchy

description: Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Site‑level preferred supplier for deliveries; logistics planning uses this link to route shipments.

Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Required for Delivery Planning Report that maps each customer site to the warehouse location used for shipments, enabling logistics scheduling and compliance with delivery windows.
Primary street address of the customer site. Used for shipment routing, hazardous materials transport documentation (DOT), and regulatory permit filings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Address; DOT 49 CFR Hazardous Materials Regulations

description: Secondary address detail for the customer site (suite, building, dock number, gate reference). Supplements address_line1 for precise delivery routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Address; Oracle Transportation Management

description: Indicates whether the site has infrastructure (tank farm, unloading arms, pumps) to receive bulk liquid or solid chemical shipments via tanker truck or rail car.
reference: SAP EWM - Storage Type Configuration; Oracle Transportation Management - Facility Capability

description: Maximum chemical storage capacity of the site expressed in metric tons. Used for supply planning, inventory replenishment modeling, and tank farm utilization analytics.
reference: SAP EWM - Storage Capacity Management; EPA SPCC Plan

description: City in which the customer site is located. Used for freight zone determination, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory reporting (EPA TRI, OSHA PSM).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Address; EPA Toxics Release Inventory (TRI) Reporting

description: Indicates whether the customer site requires a Certificate of Analysis (COA) with each chemical product shipment. Supports quality assurance, incoming inspection, and GMP compliance.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; GMP Guidelines; LabWare LIMS - COA Generation

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the customer site location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN). Drives export compliance, REACH/TSCA applicability, and international freight documentation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ECHA REACH Regulation; TSCA; DOT International Hazmat

description: Timestamp when the site record was first created in the system (ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR data retention compliance.
reference: ISO 8601; SOX Data Retention; GDPR Article 5 (Storage Limitation)

description: Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour) at which the site accepts chemical deliveries. Used for carrier scheduling, route optimization, and on-time delivery KPI measurement.
reference: Oracle Transportation Management - Delivery Window Constraints; SAP EWM

description: Earliest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour) at which the site accepts chemical deliveries. Used for carrier scheduling, route optimization, and on-time delivery KPI measurement.
reference: Oracle Transportation Management - Delivery Window Constraints; SAP EWM

description: Free-text description of physical dock constraints at the site (e.g., maximum trailer length, dock height limitations, no live unload, appointment required). Used by Oracle TMS and carrier dispatch.
reference: Oracle Transportation Management - Facility Notes; SAP EWM - Dock Management

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Contact Name
description: Name of the designated EHS contact at the customer site. Required for hazardous chemical deliveries, SDS acknowledgment, emergency response coordination, and OSHA PSM compliance.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM; EPA RMP; GHS; Intelex EHS Management

business_glossary_term: Environment Health and Safety (EHS) Contact Phone
description: Direct phone number for the site EHS contact. Used for emergency chemical incident response, hazmat delivery coordination, and regulatory inspection notifications.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM; EPA Emergency Planning and Community Right-to-Know Act (EPCRA); Intelex EHS Management

description: Fax number for the customer site. Used for transmission of SDS, COA, COC, and regulatory documentation where electronic fax is the required delivery channel.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Communication; GHS SDS Distribution Requirements

description: Indicates whether the site is certified and equipped to receive hazardous chemical materials per DOT and OSHA regulations. Governs which chemical products can be shipped to this site.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Hazardous Materials Regulations; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom; GHS

description: ICC Incoterms code defining the delivery responsibility and risk transfer point for chemical shipments to this site (e.g., DAP, DDP, FOB). Drives freight cost allocation and SAP SD billing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA SD - Incoterms; DOT Hazmat Shipping Terms

description: GPS latitude coordinate of the customer site in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for route optimization in Oracle Transportation Management, emergency response planning, and geospatial analytics.
reference: WGS 84 Coordinate System; Oracle Transportation Management - Geocoding

description: GPS longitude coordinate of the customer site in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for route optimization, emergency response planning, and geospatial analytics.
reference: WGS 84 Coordinate System; Oracle Transportation Management - Geocoding

description: Textual description of the site's standard operating hours for receiving deliveries (e.g., 'Mon-Fri 06:00-18:00 CST'). Used by Oracle TMS and SAP EWM for delivery window scheduling.
reference: Oracle Transportation Management - Delivery Window; SAP EWM - Receiving Schedule

description: Customer's preferred packaging format for chemical product receipt at this site (e.g., bulk tanker, 55-gallon drum, IBC tote, bag). Drives order fulfillment, SKU selection, and GHS labeling requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Delivery Item Category; GHS Packaging and Labelling; DOT 49 CFR

description: Expiration date of the site's primary regulatory operating or environmental permit. Triggers renewal alerts and may restrict chemical deliveries if permit lapses.
reference: EPA Permit Compliance System; OSHA PSM; Intelex EHS Management

description: Primary telephone number for the customer site. Used for delivery coordination, emergency contact, and carrier communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Communication; Salesforce CRM

description: Postal or ZIP code of the customer site. Used for freight rate calculation, carrier zone assignment, and geographic analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Address; Oracle Transportation Management

description: Name or code of the customer's preferred freight carrier for deliveries to this site. Used in Oracle TMS carrier selection and rate negotiation.
reference: Oracle Transportation Management - Carrier Preference; SAP S/4HANA SD - Shipping Conditions

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Registration Number
description: ECHA REACH registration number applicable to the customer site for EU-based locations. Required for chemical supply compliance under REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006.
reference: ECHA REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; European Chemicals Agency

description: Primary environmental or operating permit number issued to the customer site by a regulatory authority (EPA, state agency, OSHA). Required for hazardous chemical delivery authorization and compliance documentation.

description: External system identifier for this site record in Salesforce CRM (system of record). Used for data lineage, cross-system reconciliation, and integration with Salesforce-driven sales and service workflows.

description: Indicates whether the site requires a GHS-compliant Safety Data Sheet (SDS) to accompany every chemical shipment. Mandatory for hazardous chemical deliveries under OSHA HazCom and GHS standards.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom Standard; GHS; REACH Article 31

description: Primary mode of transportation used for chemical product deliveries to this site. Drives freight planning, DOT hazmat packaging requirements, and carrier assignment. LTL = Less Than Truckload, FTL = Full Truckload.

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the site, sourced from SAP S/4HANA customer master or Salesforce CRM. Used in order management, shipping documents, and COA/COC references.

description: Human-readable name of the customer site or plant location (e.g., 'Baytown Refinery', 'Chicago Distribution Center'). Used on shipping labels, SDS delivery documentation, and customer communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Name; Salesforce CRM - Account Site Name

description: Current lifecycle status of the customer site record. Controls whether orders, deliveries, and EHS documentation can be processed against this site.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Account Group Status; Salesforce CRM - Account Status

description: Classification of the physical site by its primary operational function. Drives logistics planning, hazmat receiving rules, and EHS compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_plant|distribution_center|rd_lab|warehouse|terminal|blending_facility|storage_depot|other — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Classification; Oracle Transportation Management - Location Type

description: State or province of the customer site. Used for tax jurisdiction, DOT hazmat routing restrictions, and state-level environmental permit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Customer Master Address; DOT 49 CFR; EPA State Reporting

description: Indicates whether the customer site has a dedicated tank farm for bulk liquid chemical storage. Determines eligibility for bulk tanker delivery and long-term supply agreements.
reference: SAP EWM - Storage Infrastructure; EPA SPCC (Spill Prevention Control and Countermeasure) Plan

description: Indicates whether the site requires temperature-controlled storage or delivery conditions for chemical products (e.g., cryogenic, refrigerated, or heated materials).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Storage Conditions; Oracle Transportation Management - Special Requirements

description: IANA time zone identifier for the customer site (e.g., 'America/Chicago', 'Europe/Berlin'). Used for delivery window scheduling, order cutoff time calculation, and global reporting normalization.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; Oracle Transportation Management - Scheduling

description: UN/LOCODE five-character location code identifying the customer site's port, city, or logistics node. Used in international shipping documentation, ASN processing, and TMS routing.
reference: UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations); Oracle Transportation Management

description: Description of unloading equipment available at the site (e.g., forklift, pump, conveyor, crane, vacuum system). Ensures carrier and driver preparedness for chemical product offloading.

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the site record (ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture, audit trail, and master data governance.
